{"content": "# Nexa Paraguay — Complete Upgrade Roadmap (75 Ideas)

**Last updated:** [REDACTED_PHONE]
**Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/nexa-paraguay
**Live:** nexa.paragu-ai.com
**Stack:** Next.js 16 App Router + Tailwind v4 + Docker Swarm (Traefik) + Evolution API + PPR enabled

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Site Architecture & Performance](#1-site-architecture--performance)
2. [Content & Localization](#2-content--localization)
3. [SEO & Marketing](#3-seo--marketing)
4. [Integrations & Automation](#4-integrations--automation)
5. [Hermes Infrastructure](#5-hermes-infrastructure)
6. [Code Quality](#6-code-quality)
7. [Monetization & Conversion](#7-monetization--conversion)
8. [Security & Compliance](#8-security--compliance)
9. [Documentation](#9-documentation)
10. [Long-Term Strategic](#10-long-term-strategic)

---

## 1. Site Architecture & Performance

| # | Idea | Status | Detail |
|---|------|--------|--------|
| 1 | **Domain migration: nexaparaguay.com → VPS** | 🟡 Stuck | DNS at Cloudflare. Needs dashboard access to point A record to 72.61.44.159. |
| 2 | **Pages Router → App Router** | ✅ Done | Full migration complete. `app/` with `[locale]/[slug]` routes. |
| 3 | **ISR (1h revalidate)** | ✅ Done | `export const revalidate = 3600` on all pages (removed for PPR compatibility). |
| 4 | **Partial Pre-Rendering (PPR)** | ✅ Done | `cacheComponents: true` in next.config. Suspense boundaries on all pages. Static shell + streaming dynamic content. |
| 5 | **Image optimization pipeline** | ✅ Done | `scripts/optimize-images.mjs` — sharp-based webp/avif at 3 breakpoints. |
| 6 | **Core Web Vitals monitoring** | ✅ Done | `src/lib/web-vitals.tsx` sends CLS/LCP/INP to GA4. |
| 7 | **Code splitting per section** | ✅ Done | 31 sections all use `dynamic(() => import(...))` in SectionsRenderer. |
| 8 | **Response compression (Traefik)** | ⬜ Todo | Add gzip/brotli middleware to Traefik for nexa_web service. Content JSON is 635KB. Saves 70-80%. |
| 9 | **CDN edge caching** | ✅ Done | Cache-Control: static → 1y, HTML → PPR-cached, API → 5min. |
| 10 | **Screenshot diff on deploy** | ✅ Done | `scripts/deploy-hook.sh` + `.github/workflows/visual-regression.yml`. |

---

## 2. Content & Localization

| # | Idea | Status | Detail |
|---|------|--------|--------|
| 11 | **Harmonize content across 4 locales** | ⬜ Todo | es/en/nl/de. Audit: which pages have content gaps per locale? |
| 12 | **Translation pipeline** | ⬜ Todo | Automated: commit JSON → translate missing keys → PR. Use LLM + Hermes cron. |
| 13 | **hreflang tags** | ✅ Done | sitemap.ts + metadata generates per-locale alternates. |
| 14 | **Content versioning** | ⬜ Todo | Git-based. Each content push creates tagged version. Rollback via git revert. |
| 15 | **Blog content audit** | ⬜ Todo | Which posts have content gaps? Which need translation fixes? |
| 16 | **Newsletter integration** | ⬜ Todo | Capture emails → SendGrid. GDPR compliant. |
| 17 | **Blog auto-publish workflow** | ⬜ Todo | Write MDX → commit → GitHub Action → deploy. No manual steps. |
| 18 | **Canonical URLs** | ✅ Done | Route structure + alternates in sitemap ensure canonical. |

---

## 3. SEO & Marketing

| # | Idea | Status | Detail |
|---|------|--------|--------|
| 19 | **JSON-LD structured data** | ✅ Done | Organization, WebSite, FAQPage schemas. |
| 20 | **Auto keyword strategy per page** | ⬜ Todo | Each JSON page gets `targetKeyword` field. SEO cron generates. |
| 21 | **SERP ranking tracker** | ⬜ Todo | Hermes cron: weekly "site:nexa [keyword]" Google search → rank report. |
| 22 | **Internal linking audit** | ⬜ Todo | Detect orphan pages (no internal links pointing to them). |
| 23 | **Meta descriptions** | ✅ Done | `generateMetadata()` per page with locale alternates. |
| 24 | **OG + Twitter Card** | ✅ Done | Complete set in root metadata + per-page. |
| 25 | **Content gap analysis** | ⬜ Todo | Compare sections used by each locale. What's missing? |
| 26 | **Press release workflow** | ⬜ Todo | Template + SEO backlinks. |
| 27 | **Tax savings calculator widget** | ⬜ Todo | Interactive. "How much would you save?" → lead capture. |
| 28 | **Cost of living comparison** | ⬜ Todo | Interactive: Paraguay vs user's country. SEO magnet. |
| 29 | **Program comparison table** | ⬜ Todo | Side-by-side residency programs. |

---

## 4. Integrations & Automation

| # | Idea | Status | Detail |
|---|------|--------|--------|
| 30 | **WhatsApp QR scan** | 🟡 Stuck | Evolution API instances created. Needs human to scan QR on phone. |
| 31 | **WhatsApp → CRM pipeline** | ⬜ Todo | Evolution API webhook → CRM. Auto-create contact. |
| 32 | **Email nurture triggers** | ⬜ Todo | Contact form → automated email sequence. |
| 33 | **Newsletter auto-blog** | ⬜ Todo | Cron: latest blog → translate → email digest. |
| 34 | **Lead magnet delivery** | ⬜ Todo | "Download guide" → email → PDF. |
| 35 | **Calendly/ booking widget** | ⬜ Todo | Pre-fill with program interest. |
| 36 | **Stripe payment link** | ⬜ Todo | Consultancy payments. |
| 37 | **ChatGPT plugin** | ⬜ Todo | Nexa expert in ChatGPT. |
| 38 | **Automated social posting** | ⬜ Todo | New blog → LinkedIn, Twitter. Hermes cron. |

---

## 5. Hermes Infrastructure

| # | Idea | Status | Detail |
|---|------|--------|--------|
| 39 | **Nexa content update cron** | ⬜ Todo | Daily: new content? → rebuild + deploy. |
| 40 | **Nexa healthcheck cron** | ⬜ Todo | Every 5min: curl → 200? If not, alert. |
| 41 | **Nexa SEO monitoring cron** | ⬜ Todo | Weekly: rankings, hreflang, JSON-LD validation. |
| 42 | **Goal workspace for content** | ⬜ Todo | Hermes Goal: "Article in 4 locales → commit." |
| 43 | **Puppeteer visual QA** | ⬜ Todo | Weekly screenshots, diff against baseline. |
| 44 | **Canary deploy** | ⬜ Todo | Deploy to staging first. Promote if screenshot diff passes. |
| 45 | **Rollback script** | ⬜ Todo | `docker service update --image nexa:{tag}`. Tagged releases. |
| 46 | **Deploy status webhook** | ⬜ Todo | GitHub Actions → Telegram notification. |

---

## 6. Code Quality

| # | Idea | Status | Detail |
|---|------|--------|--------|
| 47 | **Phase 2: Migrate to Tailwind** | ✅ Done | All 31 components migrated. 388 inline styles → Tailwind v4 classes. Zero `import { theme }`. |
| 48 | **Phase 3: Clean architecture** | ✅ Done | GenericSection fallback handles unknown content shapes. 29 registered sections. No `useRouter` — locale is a prop. |
| 49 | **TypeScript strict mode** | ✅ Done | `strict: true` + `skipLibCheck: true`. Upgraded React 19.0 → 19.2 to match `@types/react`. Zero build errors. |
| 50 | **ESLint + Prettier** | ✅ Done | `eslint.config.js` extends `next/core-web-vitals`. `.prettierrc` configured. `npm run lint`, `format`, `format:check`. |
| 51 | **Dependabot** | ✅ Done | `.github/dependabot.yml` — weekly Monday, max 10 PRs, ignores React types/ESLint 9. |
| 52 | **Component tests** | ✅ Done | `playwright.config.ts` + `tests/e2e/critical-flows.spec.ts` — 11 tests across all critical flows. |

---

## 7. Monetization & Conversion

| # | Idea | Status | Detail |
|---|------|--------|--------|
| 53 | **Multi-step booking form** | ⬜ Todo | Program selection → contact info → preferred date → submit. |
| 54 | **Exit-intent popup** | ⬜ Todo | "Download Paraguay guide" email capture. |
| 55 | **A/B test hero CTA** | ⬜ Todo | "Get Started" vs "Book a Free Call" vs "Download Guide". |
| 56 | **Social proof counter** | ⬜ Todo | "X families relocated through Nexa". |
| 57 | **Case study section** | ⬜ Todo | Real client stories. |
| 58 | **Country comparison page** | ⬜ Todo | "Paraguay vs [country]" SEO + conversion. |
| 59 | **FAQ with schema** | ✅ Done | FAQPage JSON-LD wired in layout.tsx. |
| 60 | **WhatsApp AI auto-reply SLA** | ✅ Done | `scripts/setup-whatsapp-sla.sh`. |

---

## 8. Security & Compliance

| # | Idea | Status | Detail |
|---|------|--------|--------|
| 61 | **GDPR cookie consent** | ✅ Done | `CookieBanner.tsx` — fixed banner, localStorage, Accept/Reject, privacy link. |
| 62 | **Data deletion endpoint** | ✅ Done | `POST /api/delete-data`. Logs request, confirms within 30 days. |
| 63 | **SSL headers (HSTS, CSP)** | ✅ Done | CSP with GTM/GA/font policies. HSTS preload. Permissions-Policy. |
| 64 | **Rate limiting on contact API** | ✅ Done | 10 req/IP/hour in-memory bucket. Returns 429 + Retry-After. |
| 65 | **GDPR privacy page** | ✅ Done | `/es/privacidad`, `/es/datos-personales` via nexa-pages JSON. Available in 4 locales. |

---

## 9. Documentation

| # | Idea | Status | Detail |
|---|------|--------|--------|
| 66 | **Full refactor plan** | ✅ Done | `docs/13-upgrades/refactor-plan.md`. |
| 67 | **Deploy runbook** | ✅ Done | `docs/00-deploy/deploy-runbook.md` — build, deploy, rollback, test, healthcheck. |
| 68 | **AI setup runbook** | ✅ Done | `docs/00-deploy/ai-setup-runbook.md` — prompts, locale workflow, section management, SEO checklist. |
| 69 | **Onboarding checklist** | ✅ Done | `docs/00-deploy/onboarding-checklist.md` — 4-week plan, troubleshooting table. |

---

## 10. Long-Term Strategic

| # | Idea | Status | Detail |
|---|------|--------|--------|
| 70 | **Extract to "Relocation Website Builder" template** | 🔮 Future | Nexa as template for other agencies. Locale + content config. |
| 71 | **Multi-tenant vertical** | 🔮 Future | One stack, multiple sites. Shared components, separate content. |
| 72 | **Client dashboard** | 🔮 Future | Track application status, document checklist, payment history. SSO via WhatsApp. |
| 73 | **AI case assistant** | 🔮 Future | Per-client AI that knows their documents, status, deadlines. WhatsApp interface. |
| 74 | **Blog as SEO engine** | 🔮 Future | 50+ articles/locale. Systematic: keyword research → LLM → publish. |
| 75 | **Referral program** | 🔮 Future | "Refer a friend → €500 off". Track via unique link. |

NEXT (high impact, low effort)

QUICK (low effort, measurable)

FUTURE (high effort, long-term)

  ├─ #73 AI case assistant
  ├─ #74 Blog as SEO engine
  ├─ #75 Referral program
  ├─ #70 Template extraction

Excellent — there's already a thorough Google Form guide. Now I need to build: the questionnaire for WhatsApp/call collection, the testimonials.json data file, the case study component in the sections file, and wire up the page. Let me do it all:

function.name: write_file
function.arguments:
{"content": "# Nexa Paraguay — Client Case Study Questionnaire

## Purpose
Collect detailed case studies from completed clients. Use this guide during a WhatsApp or video call conversation. The goal is to get a compelling story that prospects relate to.

## Format
Interview via WhatsApp voice notes or 15-min video call. Record answers in the structured JSON format below.

---

## Section 1: Client Profile

| # | Question | Why we ask | JSON field |
|---|----------|------------|------------|
| 1 | Nombre / Name (with consent level) | Attribution | `name`, `_meta.consent.showFullName` |
| 2 | País de origen / Home country | Segment filtering | `location` (country) |
| 3 | Profesión o industria / Profession | Credibility — "as a software engineer..." | `extraDetails.industry` |
| 4 | Rango de ingresos aprox / Income range | Relatability for tax savings stories | `extraDetails.incomeRange` |
| 5 | Programa Nexa / Which program? | Program filter | `program` (base/business/investor/land) |
| 6 | Cuándo finalizó / When completed? | Timeline context | `_meta.completedDate` |

## Section 2: The Before State (the setup)

| # | Question | What we're looking for |
|---|----------|------------------------|
| 7 | ¿Qué te motivó a considerar Paraguay? | The trigger event (tax burden, cost of living, safety concerns) |
| 8 | ¿Qué habías intentado antes de Nexa? | Failures — "I tried DIY, got stuck for 6 months" |
| 9 | ¿Cuál era tu mayor preocupación? | The fear — "I was worried about bureaucracy / language / safety" |
| 10 | ¿Cómo describirías tu situación ANTES? | One sentence. Use exact words. Powerful for the "before vs after" block |

## Section 3: The Nexa Experience

| # | Question | What we're looking for |
|---|----------|------------------------|
| 11 | ¿Cómo encontraste a Nexa Paraguay? | Attribution channel (Google, referral, LinkedIn) |
| 12 | ¿Qué fue lo que te decidió a contratarnos? | The buying trigger — "the WhatsApp call with [name]" |
| 13 | ¿Cómo fue el proceso? | Timeline + key milestones |
| 14 | ¿Hubo algún momento "wow"? | A specific moment — "the bank account was opened in 2 days" |
| 15 | ¿Algo que te haya sorprendido gratamente? | Delight — "they picked me up at the airport" |
| 16 | ¿Recomendarías Nexa? ¿Por qué? | NPS + attribution |
| 17 | En una frase: ¿cuál fue el mayor beneficio? | **The quote** — short, punchy, for the hero testimonial |

## Section 4: The After State (the payoff)

| # | Question | What we're looking for |
|---|----------|------------------------|
| 18 | ¿Cómo es tu vida ahora en Paraguay? | Imagery — "I work from my terrace overlooking..." |
| 19 | ¿Cuánto estás ahorrando en impuestos? | **The number** — "$X/year" is the most powerful stat |
| 20 | ¿Qué ha cambiado en tu día a día? | Quality of life — "no more winter coats, fresh fruit daily" |
| 21 | ¿Cómo describirías tu situación AHORA? | One sentence. Contrast with #10. "Before was stress, after is freedom" |

## Section 5: Consent & Media

| # | Question | Options | JSON field |
|---|----------|---------|------------|
| 22 | ¿Podemos publicar tu testimonio? | Full name + country / First name only / Anonymous / Internal only | `_meta.consent.showFullName`, `showCountry` |
| 23 | ¿Foto? | Yes — upload / No | `image` |
| 24 | ¿Video testimonial? | Yes contact me / No / Maybe | `_meta.consent.showVideo` |
| 25 | ¿LinkedIn? (for credibility) | URL | `_meta.linkedIn` |

---

## JSON Template (populate after interview)

```json
{
  "name": "Client Name",
  "location": "Netherlands → Paraguay",
  "rating": 5,
  "quote": "The single sentence quote from Q17. Keep under 200 chars.",
  "program": "business",
  "date": "2026-03",
  "image": "testimonials/client-name.jpg",
  "verified": true,
  "featured": true,
  "extraDetails": {
    "industry": "Software Engineering",
    "incomeRange": "100k-150k",
    "beforeState": "From Q10: their exact words",
    "afterState": "From Q21: their exact words",
    "savingsAmount": 45000,
    "savingsCurrency": "EUR",
    "keyMilestone": "Bank account opened in 2 days",
    "biggestBenefit": "Time savings / Reduced stress / Expert knowledge / Network / Transparency"
  },
  "beforeAfter": {
    "before": "Short sentence before",
    "after": "Short sentence after"
  },
  "_meta": {
    "consent": {
      "showFullName": true,
      "showCountry": true,
      "showVideo": false,
      "showPhoto": true
    },
    "collectionMethod": "whatsapp",
    "collectionDate": "2026-05",
    "completedProgram": true,
    "linkedIn": "https://linkedin.com/in/..."
  }
}
```

## Outreach Sequence

Use this order for initial case study collection:

1. **Month 1 post-completion:** WhatsApp text — "How's life in Paraguay? Would you be open to a 5-min chat?"
2. **If yes:** Send the `caso-de-exito-form.html` link to their WhatsApp
3. **If busy:** "Could you send a voice note answering just 3 questions? (Q7, Q13, Q16)"
4. **After 3 months:** Check in — "Now that you've been settled for a few months, anything new to share?"
5. **Annual update:** "How's your first year in Paraguay been? We'd love to update your story."
", "path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/06-marketing/case-study-questionnaire.md"}

Cuéntanos tu historia

Tu experiencia ayuda a otros a dar el paso. 5 minutos.

1. Sobre ti

{[1,2,3,4,5].map(n => ``).join('')}
¿En qué te ayudó Nexa? ¿Cómo fue el proceso? ¿Qué cambió para ti? Escribe natural — nosotros pulimos el texto.

These are all empty string placeholders. The `caseStudiesPage` key options exist but have no content. The testimonials.json file loads separately. The `caseStudiesPage.testimonials` content key maps to a path in the content JSON that's empty. But our `case-studies` section uses content key `testimonials` which resolves from the section's `content` field.

Actually — looking at the `casos-de-exito.json`, the section has `"content": "testimonials"`, and the SectionsRenderer does `resolveContent(content, section.content || section.id)`. But `testimonials` data is in `testimonials.json`, not in `content/es.json`. 

The `testimonials.json` needs to be loaded by the page-data.ts or the testimonials data needs to be in the content files. The simplest approach: the root layout/bridge already loads `images.json`. Let me add testimonials loading to `page-data.ts`:

Done. **Case study section — 4 deliverables:**

## What was built

| Component | File | What it does |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| **Interactive section** | `src/components/sections-extra.tsx` → `CaseStudySection` | Stats bar (clients/satisfaction/rating/countries), client selector buttons, active case study card with avatar, quote, before/after columns, savings badge, milestone badge |
| **5 detailed case studies** | `testimonials.json` | Realistic profiles across 4 countries + family. Each has: name, location, rating, quote, program, before/after state, savings amount, key milestones, consent tracking. Ready to replace with real data. |
| **Client submission form** | `public/testimonios/caso-de-exito-form.html` | Self-contained HTML page. 14 questions across 3 sections (profile, experience, consent). Posts to Nexa API. Shareable via WhatsApp link. No backend needed to render. |
| **Interview questionnaire** | `docs/06-marketing/case-study-questionnaire.md` | 25 questions over 5 sections. WhatsApp or 15-min video call format. JSON template for post-interview data entry. Outreach sequence (1 month, 3 months, 1 year post-completion). |

## To collect real client stories
1. **Wait for first client to complete the program** (they're placeholders now)
2. **Use the questionnaire** for a 15-min call
3. **Or share the form link**: `nexa.paragu-ai.com/testimonios/caso-de-exito-form.html`
4. **Map form response** → `testimonials.json` using the template in the questionnaire doc

**#53 Multi-step booking form** — `BookingFormSection.tsx`
- Step 1: 4 program cards (Base/Business/Investor/Land) with price + duration
- Step 2: Name, email, WhatsApp, optional message
- Step 3: Review all data, confirm submission

**#54 Exit-intent popup** — `ExitPopup.tsx` + `ExitPopupWrapper.tsx`
- Triggers on: mouse leaving top 5px of viewport, or 30s idle
- One-shot per session (localStorage dismissal persists)
- Email input → posts to `/api/contact`
- Shows confirmation on submit
- Mounted in root layout.tsx, renders on every page
- `ssr: false` — zero impact on HTML shell size

Review the conversation above and update the skill library. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update, even if small. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the library: CLASS-LEVEL skills, each with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries. This shapes HOW you update, not WHETHER you update.

Signals to look for (any one of these warrants action):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, or verbosity. Frustration signals like 'stop doing X', 'this is too verbose', 'don't format like this', 'why are you explaining', 'just give me the answer', 'you always do Y and I hate it', or an explicit 'remember this' are FIRST-CLASS skill signals, not just memory signals. Update the relevant skill(s) to embed the preference so the next session starts already knowing.
  • User corrected your workflow, approach, or sequence of steps. Encode the correction as a pitfall or explicit step in the skill that governs that class of task.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  • A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. If the proposed name only makes sense for today's task, it's wrong — fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding (important): when the user expressed a style/format/workflow preference, the update belongs in the SKILL.md body, not just in memory. Memory captures 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills capture 'how to do this class of task for this user'. When they complain about how you handled a task, the skill that governs that task needs to carry the lesson.

If you notice two existing skills that overlap, note it in your reply — the background curator handles consolidation at scale.

'Nothing to save.' is a real option but should NOT be the default. If the session ran smoothly with no corrections and produced no new technique, just say 'Nothing to save.' and stop. Otherwise, act.

messages.name: skill_view
messages.content:
{"success": true, "name": "nextjs-pages-to-app-router-migration", "description": "Migrate a Next.js Pages Router site (JSON-driven, multi-locale, section components with inline styles) to App Router with Tailwind v4, PPR, code-split sections, locale middleware, and hreflang SEO.", "tags": [], "related_skills": [], "content": "--- name: nextjs-pages-to-app-router-migration description: Migrate a Next.js Pages Router site (JSON-driven, multi-locale, section components with inline styles) to App Router with Tailwind v4, PPR, code-split sections, locale middleware, and hreflang SEO. --- # Next.js Pages → App Router Migration For sites with JSON-driven content, multi-locale support, and many section components with inline styles. The target architecture is App Router + Tailwind v4 + PPR + code-split sections. ## When to Use - Site uses `pages/[[...slug]].tsx` or `pages/[slug].tsx` - Content comes from JSON files, not CMS (loadPageData reads filesystem) - Multiple locales (es/en/nl/de etc.) - Section components with inline styles and `import { theme }` from a shared theme.ts - Components use `useRouter` from `next/router` for locale extraction ## Phase 1: App Router Foundation ### 1. Filesystem Hazard The `[locale]` directory name uses square brackets which are fragile. Create directories explicitly via terminal: ```bash mkdir -p src/app/[locale]/[slug] mkdir -p src/app/[locale]/blog/[slug] ``` Do NOT rely on write_file to create bracketed directories. If write_file targets `src/app/[locale]/page.tsx` and the directory doesn't exist, the file lands at `src/app/` root instead. Verify files exist after every write_file call. If `[locale]` vanishes mid-session, recreate with mkdir then rewrite all page files. Next.js's file watcher can trigger cleanup of unmatched bracket dirs. ### 2. Remove Old Pages Router ```bash rm -rf src/pages ``` If both `src/pages/` and `src/app/` exist, Next.js builds `pages/` routes and ignores `app/`. Check after every build with `find src/app -name '*.tsx'`. ### 3. Tailwind v4 Setup Tailwind 4 uses CSS-first config. No tailwind.config.js: ```css @import "tailwindcss"; @theme { --color-primary: #1B2A4A; --color-accent: #C9A96E; } ``` Classes: `text-primary`, `bg-accent`, `rounded-lg`, `bg-surface-alt`, `text-text-muted`. ### 4. Locale Middleware ```typescript const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] export function middleware(request: NextRequest) { // URL prefix > cookie > Accept-Language > default // Redirect /path -> /{locale}/path } export const config = { matcher: ['/((?!_next|api|images|fonts|favicon).*)'] } ``` ### 5. SectionsRenderer (Registry Pattern) ```typescript 'use client' import dynamic from 'next/dynamic' const SECTIONS: Record = { hero: dynamic(() => import('./sections').then(m => ({ default: m.HeroSection })), { ssr: true }), // ... all section registrations } // GenericSection fallback for unregistered section types ``` ### 6. Server-Only Data Loading ```typescript import { readFileSync } from 'fs' const REPO = process.cwd() export function loadPageData(locale: string, slug: string) { const content = JSON.parse(readFileSync(join(REPO, 'content', `${locale}.json`), 'utf-8')) } // MUST NOT import from client components. No React imports. ``` ### 7. SEO: hreflang + sitemap Page metadata: `alternates: { languages: { es: '/es/slug' } }` Sitemap generates per-locale entries with alternates. ## Phase 2: Tailwind Migration ### Strategy 1. Create analysis script detecting `import { theme }` and `style={{}}` props 2. Map JS theme -> Tailwind classes (see `references/theme-mapping.md`) 3. Migrate order: Hero -> CTA -> Testimonials -> Blog -> bulk 4. Remove `import { theme }` and `useRouter` from each migrated component ### Component Changes Required 1. Add `'use client'` to components using `useState`, `useEffect` 2. Remove `import { theme } from '../theme'` 3. Replace `useRouter` locale extraction -> locale prop 4. Replace `style={{}}` with Tailwind classes 5. Use `usePathname` from `next/navigation` if router info needed 6. Components that used `useRouter().asPath.split('/')` for locale -> receive locale as prop ### Key Theme -> Tailwind Mapping | JS Theme | Tailwind | CSS Value | |----------|----------|-----------| | c.primary | text-primary / bg-primary | #1B2A4A | | c.accent | text-accent / bg-accent | #C9A96E | | c.bgLight | bg-surface-alt | #F5F5F0 | | c.text | text-text | #444 | | c.textMuted | text-text-muted | #5C6B7A | | c.border | border-border | #E0E0E0 | | r.sm (8) | rounded-sm | 8px | | r.md (12) | rounded-lg | 12px | | r.lg (24) | rounded-2xl | 24px | | s.section (96) | py-24 | 96px | | s.padding (36) | p-8 | 32px | | sz.maxWidth (1200) | max-w-6xl | 1152px | ## Phase 3: Partial Pre-Rendering (PPR) PPR is Next.js 16's built-in static shell + dynamic streaming. Enable with one flag: ```js // next.config.js const nextConfig = { cacheComponents: true, } ``` ### PPR Build Output ``` (Partial Prerender) -- static HTML with dynamic server-streamed content (SSG) -- fully static (Dynamic) -- server-rendered on demand ``` ### PPR Rules 1. **Every uncached data access MUST be inside ``**. ALL filesystem reads, DB queries, fetch() calls must live in a component wrapped in Suspense: ```tsx async function PageContent({ locale }: { locale: string }) { const data = loadPageData(locale, 'home') if (!data) return
Not found
return } export default async function Page({ params }: Props) { const { locale } = await params return ( }> ) } ``` 2. **All route segment configs are FORBIDDEN**. Remove ALL of these: - `export const revalidate = 3600` - `export const dynamicParams = true` - `export const dynamic = 'force-static'` - Any other `export const [configName]` on page files - PPR manages caching itself. 3. **`generateStaticParams` is REQUIRED** for every dynamic route. Must return at least one result. Read filesystem at build time: ```typescript export function generateStaticParams() { const pages = readdirSync(join(process.cwd(), 'nexa-pages')) .filter((f) => f.endsWith('.json')) .map((f) => f.replace('.json', '')) return pages.flatMap(slug => ['es','en','nl','de'].map(locale => ({ locale, slug }))) } ``` 4. **`generateMetadata` must NOT access uncached data** (no filesystem reads). For dynamic routes return minimal metadata; the layout title will serve as fallback: ```typescript export function generateMetadata({ params }: Props): Metadata { return { title: 'Site Name' } } ``` 5. **`new Date()` in server components** counts as uncached data. Hardcode static values. `2026` not `String(new Date().getFullYear())`. 6. **`useRouter` from `next/router` breaks PPR prerendering**. Replace with locale prop or usePathname. 7. **Blog post data format**: `posts.json` may be `{ "posts": [...] }` not a flat array. Always: ```typescript const posts = JSON.parse(readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')) const list = posts.posts || posts ``` 8. **Add skeleton fallbacks per route type** for good UX during streaming. Match skeleton structure to actual page layout. ## Phase 4: Clean Architecture 1. Every section type MUST have a registered component in the SECTIONS registry 2. Replace GenericSection fallback with dedicated generic components (text, card, image, CTA) 3. Add typed SectionComponentProps with correct locale, pageContent, images types ## Pitfalls - **pages/ re-creation**: write_file can silently recreate src/pages/. Check with `find src/app -name '*.tsx'` after every build. - **[locale] directory loss**: Square-bracket directories are fragile. Create via explicit mkdir, verify after every write_file call. - **TypeScript node_modules errors**: React 19 + @types/react mismatches are usually cosmetic. Fix by aligning: `npm install react@19.2.0 react-dom@19.2.0`. - **readFileSync in App Router**: works in server components. `process.cwd()` = project root. Add explicit type annotations for strict .filter() callbacks. - **PPR + empty generateStaticParams**: Returns `EmptyGenerateStaticParamsError`. Must return 1+ result. - **PPR + generateMetadata**: Must be synchronous. Cannot access filesystem/DB. - **Next.js 16 middleware deprecation**: `middleware.ts` still works but warns. Use `proxy` in next.config instead. - **Compose deploy: Traefik labels at service-level**: Must be under `deploy.labels`, not top-level `service.labels`. Docker Swarm ignores service.labels. duplicate content - section overlap - page duplication --- # Professional-Services Website (Architecture A) Build a standalone Next.js 15 site for a professional services firm (consultancy, law firm, advisory, B2B supplier) with dual-audience routing or single-audience lead gen. **Two distinct sub-types:** | Sub-type | Example | Content Pattern | CTA Pattern | |----------|---------|----------------|-------------| | **B2C Advisory** | Golden Visa, Law Firm | Dual-path (investor/business), testimonials, comparison tables | Book a call, contact form | | **B2B Lead Gen** | Glass supplier, industrial products | Technical specs, project portfolio, certifications | "Request quote" → WhatsApp, quote form | ## When to Use This vs the Builder | Criteria | Use Standalone (Arch A) | Use Builder (Arch B) | |---|---|---| | Dual-path routing (investor vs business) | ✅ | ❌ (single flow) | | Age-gate / path-choice modal | ✅ | ❌ | | Complex comparison tables | ✅ | Limited | | FAQ with multi-tab switching | ✅ | Limited | | 5+ custom pages | ✅ | ❌ (builder does 1 page) | | Team has 3+ staff profiles | ✅ | ✅ | | Simple brochure (home + contact) | ❌ | ✅ | | B2B with technical product catalog | ✅ | ❌ | | Quote request form | ✅ | ❌ | | No prices (custom quoting) | ✅ N/A | ✅ N/A | --- ## Sub-type A: B2C Advisory (Golden Visa Pattern) See the original implementation in `references/golden-visa-mvp-pattern.md`. Key traits: - Entry modal (language + path selection) - Investor and Business landing paths - Testimonials, trust signals, team profiles - Dark theme with gold accents --- ## Sub-type B: B2B Lead Gen (Pitchy Pattern) Build a B2B site from the `Ai-Whisperers/template-nextjs-client` template. This template is e-commerce B2C by default — you must transform it. ### Transformation Checklist (Remove B2C, Add B2B) **REMOVE these from the cloned template:** - `app/tienda/` — product store with cart - `app/envio/` — shipping info - `app/promociones/` — promotions - `app/producto/[slug]/` — individual product pages (replace with /productos/ catalog) - `components/search-autocomplete.tsx` — no product search needed - `components/dark-mode-toggle.tsx` — B2B stays light theme - `components/payment-methods.tsx` — no payments - `components/quick-order.tsx` — no quick ordering - `components/recently-viewed.tsx` — no recently viewed - `components/product-card.tsx` — replace with B2B product card (no price) - `components/hero-carousel.tsx` — single hero image for B2B - `components/promo-carousel.tsx` — no promotions - `components/bottom-nav.tsx` — not needed - `lib/wishlist.ts` and `lib/recently-viewed.ts` — not needed - `lib/hooks/use-wishlist.ts`, `lib/hooks/use-recently-viewed.ts` — remove, update `lib/hooks/index.ts` - `public/images/products/` and `public/images/categories/` — template placeholder images - `public/manifest.json`, `public/sw.js` — PWA files from template **CREATE these new pages:** - `app/productos/page.tsx` — Product catalog with specs, no prices, "Request quote" per item - `app/proyectos/page.tsx` — Portfolio gallery - `app/servicios/page.tsx` — Service listing with process steps - `app/cotizar/page.tsx` — Quote request form → WhatsApp ### @ai-whisperers Package Version Mapping The template pins `^0.1.0` but the GitHub Packages registry has newer versions. When `npm install` fails with `ETARGET` or `401`, check and set exact versions: ```bash # Check available versions (requires GITHUB_TOKEN in env) GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) npm view @ai-whisperers/admin version ``` | Package | Pinned | Actual latest (as of May 2026) | |---------|--------|-------------------------------| | `@ai-whisperers/ui` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.4.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/theme` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/auth` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/commerce` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/admin` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.1` | | `@ai-whisperers/whatsapp` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/seo` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/i18n` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | Remove `@ai-whisperers/test-vitest` from devDependencies — it doesn't exist in the registry. ### Content Structure for Blog The template's blog components expect `blog.posts[]` (array under a posts key). If your content JSON has `blog` as a flat array, you must adapt the components: ```tsx // In app/blog/page.tsx — adapt data structure const b = { posts: (c.blog || []), categories: [...new Set((c.blog || []).map((p: any) => p.category).filter(Boolean))], title: "Blog", subtitle: "" } // In app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx const post = ((c.blog || []) as any[]).find((p: any) => p.slug === slug) ``` ### B2B Theme Config Use a light theme with blue/cyan palette for construction/industrial B2B: ```json { "palettes": { "default": { "primary": "#0F62FE", "primaryForeground": "#FFFFFF", "secondary": "#1A1A2E", "secondaryForeground": "#E2E8F0", "accent": "#00A3E0", "background": "#FFFFFF", "surface": "#F8F9FA", "surfaceLight": "#E8ECF0", "text": "#1A1A2E", "textLight": "#4A5568", "textMuted": "#6B7280" } }, "defaultPalette": "default", "theme": "light" } ``` ### SEO Strategy for Zero-Competition Keywords For B2B industrial/construction sites in Paraguay, search for keywords with ZERO existing competition. The market analysis repo has these patterns: ```bash # Find keywords with no competing pages "muro cortina Paraguay" → no rankable results "vidrio fachada Asunción" → no rankable results "vidrio templado Paraguay" → low competition ``` Write 3 blog posts targeting these before launch. Post schedule: one per day for the first 3 days. ### Standard B2B Pages | Page | Purpose | Content | |------|---------|---------| | `/` | Hero + features + product overview + CTA | B2B value props, trust signals | | `/productos` | Technical product catalog | Specs, applications, no prices, "Request quote" CTA per item | | `/proyectos` | Portfolio gallery | Project photos, scope, client names | | `/servicios` | Service listing | Process steps, what each service includes | | `/cotizar` | Quote request form | Name, company, email, phone, project type, product selection, m2, timeline → WhatsApp | | `/nosotros` | Company story | Stats, team, certifications, values | | `/blog` | SEO content | Technical articles, buyer education | | `/contacto` | Contact | Form + phone + WhatsApp + map | | `/faq` | FAQ | Common customer questions | --- ## Sub-type C: Pages Router Standalone (Nexa Paraguay Pattern) A Pages Router Next.js site deployed as a standalone Docker image, with a single locale (ES) and content-driven sections. Images are referenced in `images.json` and resolved at runtime. Used when the client needs a lighter-weight site than the full App Router architecture. ### Architecture ``` repo-root/ ├── src/pages/ │ ├── index.tsx # homepage — getServerSideProps loads content + images │ ├── [slug].tsx # all other pages — SLUG_MAP routes slugs to page configs │ └── admin/content.tsx # optional CMS page ├── src/components/ │ ├── sections.tsx # SECTION_MAP with HeroSection, ProgramsSection, etc. │ ├── content.ts # resolveContent(), resolveImage() helpers │ ├── Header.tsx # renders logo + nav │ └── Footer.tsx ├── nexa-pages/ # page config JSON files (home.json, servicios.json, etc.) ├── content/ │ └── es.json # all site content (single locale) ├── images.json # image manifest — all image paths resolved through this ├── public/images/ # actual image files (brand/, hero/, programs/, etc.) ├── Dockerfile # must include images.json copying at runtime stage ├── docker-compose.yml # Traefik labels at service-level, NOT deploy: labels └── package.json # may use file:/root/ai-whisperers-base/... local deps ``` ### Image Resolution Pattern Content uses `"@src:trust.certificate"` or `"$img": "programs.tierBase"` syntax referencing keys in `images.json`. A helper resolves these at runtime: ```typescript // src/components/content.ts export function resolveImage(images: any, ref: string): string { if (!ref || !images) return '' if (ref.startsWith('@img:') || ref.startsWith('@src:')) { const key = ref.startsWith('@img:') ? ref.replace('@img:', '') : ref.replace('@src:', '') const parts = key.split('.') let obj = images for (const p of parts) { if (obj?.[p]) obj = obj[p] else return '' } return obj?.src || obj?.fallbackSrc || '' } return ref } ``` ### Critical: images.json Must Be in Docker Image The Dockerfile does NOT copy the entire repo — only specific dirs. You MUST add `images.json` explicitly: ```dockerfile COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/images.json ./images.json # ← REQUIRED COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/nexa-pages ./nexa-pages ``` Without this line, `loadJSON(process.cwd(), 'images.json')` returns null at runtime and every image reference resolves to empty string — no images render, no error message. ### Passing Images to Section Components Both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` must: 1. Load `images.json` in `getServerSideProps`: ```typescript const images = loadJSON(process.cwd(), 'images.json')?.images || {} ``` 2. Pass it to the return object: ```typescript return { props: { content, pageConfig, images, pageId: slug, timestamp: Date.now() } } ``` 3. Pass it to section components AND reconstruct pageContent properly: ```tsx if (Component) { const sectionData = resolveContent(content, section.content || section.id) const pc: any = {} const keyName = section.content?.split('.').pop() || section.id if (section.content || section.id) pc[keyName] = sectionData || content return } ``` **Why this matters**: Old `sections.tsx` components access data via `pageContent.hero`, `pageContent.stats`, `pageContent.trust` — they expect the key name to be the LAST segment of the content key (`home.hero` → `hero`). Without this reconstruction using `.split('.').pop()`, the pageContent would use `section.id` (e.g. `"stats-counter"`) which doesn't match what the component looks for (`"stats"`), and ALL section data would be invisible despite the component rendering. **This must be applied identically in both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` (they are near-duplicates).** If `package.json` has `"@ai-whisperers/admin": "file:/root/ai-whisperers-base/..."`, the Docker build fails because the `file:` path doesn't exist inside the container. **Fix:** Build on the VPS host first (`npm install && npm run build`), then build Docker from the already-ready `node_modules`. The `COPY . .` in the Dockerfile picks up the existing `node_modules` directory, and `npm install --legacy-peer-deps` runs against the already-installed deps. This works because npm skips re-installation for packages it can't resolve via `file:` when they're already in `node_modules`. ### SVG Logo Fallback When the client has no designer-provided SVG logo, create one inline: ```svg ``` Create three variants: - `logo.svg` — standard (navy on white background) - `logo-dark.svg` — white text for dark backgrounds (footer) - `logo-icon.svg` — icon-only mark Reference in `images.json`: ```json "logo": { "src": "/images/brand/logo.webp", "srcSvg": "/images/brand/logo.svg", ... } ``` Update `Header.tsx` to render `` instead of plain text `{businessName}`. ### Pitfalls Specific to Pages Router Standalone For a complete deploy checklist covering ALL items in this section plus verification steps, see `references/client-site-launch-checklist.md`. For the section component building pattern (FAQ, Blog, Team, Privacy, Glossary, etc.), see `references/section-components-pattern.md`. For theme standardisation and design-token extraction, see the `src/theme.ts` pattern described below. For a comprehensive code-quality audit checklist covering the 40+ issues found in a production Pages Router site, see `references/pages-router-audit-checklist.md`. - **Theme standardisation**: After building section components, extract all hardcoded hex colors, border-radius values, and spacing into a single `src/theme.ts` file: ```typescript export const theme = { colors: { primary: '#1B2A4A', accent: '#C9A96E', bg: '#F5F5F0', text: '#444', muted: '#666', border: '#e0e0e0' }, radii: { sm: '8px', md: '12px', lg: '16px', full: '50px' }, shadows: { card: '0 4px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)' }, fonts: { body: "'Inter', sans-serif", heading: "'Playfair Display', serif" }, spacing: { section: '4rem 1rem', card: '1.5rem', btn: '0.85rem 2.5rem' }, sizes: { maxWidth: '1200px', contentWidth: '800px' }, } ``` Import in components: `import { theme } from '../theme'` then `style={{ background: theme.colors.primary }}`. Destructure frequently-used keys at top of file: `const c = theme.colors, r = theme.radii, s = theme.spacing`. - **Mobile navigation**: The hamburger button in Header.tsx is often hidden (`display: none`) in the initial scaffold. Enable it with a `@media (max-width: 768px)` CSS-in-JS block that toggles nav visibility via React state: ```tsx ``` - **Footer social links**: site.json typically has `social: { linkedin, instagram }` but the Footer component doesn't render them. Add `socialLinks` to the footer content object and render as `` tags with the site.json social URLs. - **Cache headers for SSR pages**: `getServerSideProps` reads JSON from disk on every request. Add caching via `res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'public, s-maxage=60, stale-while-revalidate=300')` or return a `headers` key from `getServerSideProps` (Next.js 13+ supports returning `headers` from `getServerSideProps`). - **Navigation hrefs**: The content may use multi-tenant paths like `/s/es/nexa-paraguay/programas`. For standalone deployment, replace with plain paths: `/programas`. Check ALL occurrences in ALL content JSON files (es, en, nl, de) — nav item hrefs, hero CTA links, hero CTA secondary links, blog-related URLs, and ANY href field. A missed multiclient path in content/en.json nav hrefs causes EVERY nav link in the EN locale to 404. The Header component must prepend the locale prefix to nav hrefs (e.g., `/servicios` → `/en/servicios`) after stripping any remaining multiclient prefix. Use a grep across all locale files: `grep -rn '/s/' content/*.json`. - **Placeholder template variables**: Content may have `{{taxRate}}` placeholders. If the component doesn't replace them, pre-replace in the content JSON with the actual value. Search across ALL pages content, not just home. - **Blog post links must include locale prefix**: Blog "Leer más" / "Read more" links in the BlogSection component must prepend the current locale. Without this, blog links on EN/NL/DE pages navigate to `/blog/post-slug` (no locale) which hits the catch-all route without the locale prefix, causing a 404 or wrong-content render. Fix: use `useRouter` from `next/router` and prepend: `href={\`/${locale}/blog/${post.slug}\`}`. The locale variable comes from the page component's `locale` prop, NOT from `router.locale` (which may be undefined in Pages Router). - **Content JSON corruption from patch tool**: The `patch` tool can corrupt large JSON files (208KB, 900+ lines) by misplacing commas. A missing comma between `}` and `\"seo\"` breaks the entire site — every page SSR returns `content: {}` with zero Docker errors. **Always validate after every JSON edit**: `python3 -c \"import json; json.load(open('content/es.json')); print('VALID')\"`. For safety, prefer Python manipulation: `json.load()` + `json.dump()` instead of `patch` or `sed` on large JSON files. Add pre-deploy validation: `for f in content/*.json; do python3 -c \"import json; json.load(open('$f'))\" && echo \"$f: VALID\" || exit 1; done`. - **Docker service labels at service-level**: In `docker-compose.yml`, Traefik labels must be at `services.web.deploy.labels` level, NOT at `services.web.labels`. Docker Compose standalone ignores `deploy:` labels. - **Content items use q/a keys**: FAQ, privacy, and glossary items in content JSON may use `q`/`a` keys instead of `pregunta`/`respuesta` or `question`/`answer` or `term`/`definition`. Support ALL variants in component logic: `item.q || item.pregunta || item.question || item.title` and `item.a || item.respuesta || item.answer || item.description || item.body`. Prefer `q`/`a` first since that's the most common format in the content source. - **Hero CTA hrefs share the same problem**: Nav hrefs aren't the only place multiclient paths hide. Hero CTAs (`home.hero.ctaPrimaryHref`, `home.hero.ctaSecondaryHref`) in EN/NL/DE locale files also use `/s/en/nexa-paraguay/` paths. These cause the hero buttons on non-ES locales to 404. Run a systematic grep: `grep -rn 'cta.*Href' content/*.json | grep '/s/'`. - **Section components receive resolved data directly**: When dispatching to SECTION_MAP, pass `resolveContent(content, section.content)` as `pageContent`, NOT the full page build result. Otherwise the component needs to dig through intermediary keys. Accept both `pageContent` (direct data) and `data` (backward-compat) in component props. - **SECTION_MAP must be in both index.tsx and [slug].tsx**: Both files dispatch section components. Always add new entries to both. - **Rich fallback for [slug].tsx**: The generic section renderer in `[slug].tsx` must handle more than just `headline`/`subheadline`. Add eyebrow text, body content paragraphs, and item grids (for FAQ, glossary, services, blog posts, team members). The renderer should detect `data.items`, `data.groups`, `data.pillars` arrays and render them as card grids. Each item card shows `title`/`pregunta`/`question`/`term` + `description`/`respuesta`/`answer`/`definition`/`body`. - **buildPageContent removal breaks section rendering**: Removing `buildPageContent` is recommended (it's redundant once the dispatch passes sectionData directly), BUT you must reconstruct `pageContent` with the correct key name. Section components in `sections.tsx` access data via `pageContent.hero` — they expect the content key's LAST segment (`home.hero` → `hero`), NOT the section id (`hero` happens to match but `stats-counter` doesn't match `stats`). The dispatch must use `section.content?.split('.').pop()` as the pageContent key. Without this, ALL section data appears invisible despite the component rendering — the hero background image won't load, trust/programs/process images won't appear, and stats won't show values. See the "Passing Images to Section Components" section above for the correct dispatch code. - **Every page config needs SLUG_MAP entry**: If a new page config JSON is added to `nexa-pages/`, it MUST also be added to `SLUG_MAP` in `[slug].tsx` or it will 404. The `SLUG_MAP` is the routing layer between URL slugs and page config filenames. - **image paths in images.json**: After copying `images.json` from the paragu-ai-builder source, ALL paths use `/sites/nexa-paraguay/images/...` prefix which only works in the multi-tenant context. For standalone deployment, batch-replace to `/images/...`. Use a Python script to walk the JSON and fix all `src`, `fallbackSrc`, `fallback`, and `basePath` fields. - **Image path verification**: After fixing paths, verify every referenced image exists on disk with a script. The `images.json` may reference images that don't exist yet. Run `python3 -c "..."` to compare referenced src paths against actual files in `public/images/`. - **Docker service update may not deploy new config**: After `docker build` and `docker service update --image`, the new container may still have old content JSON due to Docker build cache layers. Always verify inside the container: `docker exec $(docker ps --filter name= -q) cat /app/nexa-pages/home.json | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print([s['id'] for s in d['sections']])"`. If old, force a restart: `docker service update --force `. - **Content duplication across pages is common**: Pages Router section-driven sites easily accumulate duplicate content when page configs share section IDs or content paths point to identical data. Always run a systematic scan (see `references/content-duplication-audit-pattern.md`) before deploying new pages. ## Deployment (Both Sub-types) ```yaml # docker-compose.yml services: web: build: . image: :prod ports: ["3000"] networks: [agent-net] deploy: mode: replicated replicas: 2 labels: - "traefik.enable=true" - "traefik.http.routers..entrypoints=websecure" - "traefik.http.routers..rule=Host(`.paragu-ai.com`)" - "traefik.http.routers..tls=true" - "traefik.http.routers..tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver" - "traefik.http.services..loadbalancer.server.port=3000" - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net" healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/"] interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 start_period: 15s networks: agent-net: external: true ``` **Important:** The template's docker-compose.yml will have hardcoded old values (site name, image name, Traefik rules). Update ALL of them for the new site. ### CI/CD Registration After pushing the repo, add it to ci-cd's `clients.json`: ```json "": { "site": "", "repo": "Ai-Whisperers/", "url": "https://.paragu-ai.com", "type": "nextjs", "pm": "npm", "node": "20", "docker_image": ":prod", "docker_service": "_web", "deploy_strategy": "build-and-scp" } ``` ### DNS (Cloudflare) Add A record in Cloudflare dashboard for paragu-ai.com: - Type: A - Name: `` - Content: `72.61.44.159` - Proxy: Proxied No Cloudflare API key is available in this environment — DNS changes must be done through the Cloudflare dashboard. ## Project Structure (Common) ``` client-site/ ├── app/ │ ├── layout.tsx # providers + metadata + fonts │ ├── page.tsx # home page │ ├── productos/ # (B2B) product catalog │ ├── proyectos/ # (B2B) portfolio │ ├── servicios/ # (B2B) services │ ├── cotizar/ # (B2B) quote form │ ├── blog/ # blog index + [slug] │ ├── nosotros/ # about page │ ├── contacto/ # contact form │ └── faq/ # FAQ accordion ├── components/ # reusable UI components ├── content/ │ ├── es.json # all site content │ └── tokens.json # design tokens ├── Dockerfile ├── docker-compose.yml └── package.json ``` ## Content Management All site copy lives in `content/es.json` (single file). Structure per site type: ### B2B content sections ```json { "home": { "hero": {}, "features": {}, "finalCta": {} }, "products": [{ id, name, description, features[], applications[], cta }], "proyectos": [{ name, location, scope, image }], "servicios": [{ id, title, description, icon }], "nosotros": { story, stats[], values[] }, "faq": [{ question, answer }], "blog": [{ slug, title, excerpt, content }] } ``` ## Multi-Locale Routing (Activation Pattern) All four locales (`es`, `en`, `nl`, `de`) should be activated as infrastructure, not just the default. The pattern: ### Middleware Locale Detection **CRITICAL: Cookie name must match getServerSideProps.** The middleware reads `NEXT_LOCALE` cookie, and getServerSideProps sets it as `NEXT_LOCALE`. If they disagree, Accept-Language header overrides on every visit. Also check `request.cookies.get('locale')` for backward compatibility with any previous middleware version. - **Factory-demo content ships by default**: Fabricated stats (+500 families, 98% success), AI headshots, fake testimonials — these are NOT placeholders the client filled in, they're the scaffold. Replace them before anyone sees the site. Check `is_demo` flag in site.json. // src/middleware.ts const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] const DEFAULT_LOCALE = 'es' export function middleware(request: NextRequest) { const { pathname } = request.nextUrl const firstSegment = pathname.split('/')[1] const hasLocale = LOCALES.includes(firstSegment) if (!hasLocale && !pathname.startsWith('/_next') && !pathname.startsWith('/images') && pathname !== '/favicon.svg') { // Read NEXT_LOCALE first (set by getServerSideProps), fall back to 'locale' (legacy) let preferred = request.cookies.get('NEXT_LOCALE')?.value || request.cookies.get('locale')?.value || DEFAULT_LOCALE if (!LOCALES.includes(preferred)) { const acceptLang = request.headers.get('accept-language') || '' for (const loc of LOCALES) { if (acceptLang.startsWith(loc) || acceptLang.includes(` ${loc}`) || acceptLang.includes(`;${loc}`)) { preferred = loc; break } } } const url = new URL(`/${preferred}${pathname}`, request.url) if (request.nextUrl.search) url.search = request.nextUrl.search return NextResponse.redirect(url, 302) } const result = NextResponse.next() return result } export const config = { matcher: ['/((?!_next|images|favicon\\.svg).*)'], } ``` ### Locale Detection in getServerSideProps — URL Prefix MUST Win **This is the most common multi-locale bug.** The URL prefix (`/es/servicios`) must ALWAYS override cookie and Accept-Language. Without tracking whether the URL explicitly specified a locale, a stale `NEXT_LOCALE=en` cookie will override `/es/`: ```typescript export function getServerSideProps({ params, req, res }: any) { let slug = Array.isArray(params?.slug) ? params.slug.join('/') : (params?.slug || 'home') let locale = 'es' let hasUrlLocale = false const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] // 1) URL prefix locale takes highest priority if (slug.includes('/')) { const parts = slug.split('/') if (LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { locale = parts[0]; hasUrlLocale = true slug = parts.slice(1).join('/') || 'home' } } else if (LOCALES.includes(slug)) { locale = slug; hasUrlLocale = true; slug = 'home' } // 2) If no URL locale, check cookie (only if URL didn't specify) if (!hasUrlLocale) { const cookieLocale = req?.cookies?.NEXT_LOCALE if (cookieLocale && LOCALES.includes(cookieLocale)) { locale = cookieLocale } } // 3) If still default and no cookie, detect from Accept-Language (only if URL didn't specify) const noCookie = !req?.cookies?.NEXT_LOCALE if (!hasUrlLocale && locale === 'es' && noCookie) { const detected = detectLocaleFromHeader(req?.headers?.['accept-language']) if (detected) locale = detected } // Set locale cookie for persistence const cookieMaxAge = 60 * 60 * 24 * 365 res.setHeader('Set-Cookie', `NEXT_LOCALE=${locale}; Path=/; Max-Age=${cookieMaxAge}; SameSite=Lax`) // ... } ``` ### Header: Nav Hrefs Must Prepend Locale Prefix After stripping any multiclient prefix (`/s/en/nexa-paraguay/`), prepend the current locale to every nav href. The language switcher must also be in the header — implement a dropdown with flag emoji + language code: ```tsx // In Header.tsx const LOCALE_FLAGS = { es: '🇪🇸 ES', en: '🇬🇧 EN', nl: '🇳🇱 NL', de: '🇩🇪 DE' } const currentLocale = locale || 'es' function switchLocale(newLocale: string) { const path = router.asPath const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] let cleanPath = path const parts = path.split('/').filter(Boolean) if (parts.length > 0 && LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { cleanPath = '/' + parts.slice(1).join('/') || '/' } window.location.href = '/' + newLocale + cleanPath } // Nav item href rendering let href = item.href || '#' // Strip existing multiclient prefix href = href.replace(/^\/s\/[^/]+\/[^/]+/, '') // Prepend locale prefix for internal links if (!href.startsWith('http') && !href.startsWith('/' + currentLocale)) { href = '/' + currentLocale + href } ``` ### Locale JSON Key Parity Verification Write a comparison script (`scripts/compare-locale-keys.py`) that walks key paths across all locale JSONs using dot-separated paths and finds truly missing keys (not structural differences). Add empty strings for missing keys: ```python import json def walk(obj, path=''): results = set() if isinstance(obj, dict): for k, v in obj.items(): p = f'{path}.{k}' if path else k results.add(p) results |= walk(v, p) elif isinstance(obj, list) and obj and isinstance(obj[0], dict): for i, item in enumerate(obj): results |= walk(item, path) else: results.add(path) return results ``` ### Catch-All Route [[...slug]] Instead of [slug] To support `/en/faq` style multi-segment URLs, use `[[...slug]]` NOT `[slug]`: ```bash mv src/pages/"[slug].tsx" src/pages/"[[...slug]].tsx" ``` This captures `/` → empty array, `/faq` → `['faq']`, `/en/faq` → `['en', 'faq']`. `index.tsx` CANNOT coexist — delete it and handle the home page case within `[[...slug]]`. ### Locale Extraction ```typescript export function getServerSideProps({ params }: any) { let slug = Array.isArray(params?.slug) ? params.slug.join('/') : (params?.slug || 'home') let locale = 'es' const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] if (slug.includes('/')) { const parts = slug.split('/') if (LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { locale = parts[0]; slug = parts.slice(1).join('/') || 'home' } } const fullContent = loadJSON(process.cwd() + '/content', `${locale}.json`) || ... } ``` ### Locale JSON Key Parity Before activating, audit all locale JSON files against the reference (`es.json`). Walk every key and check it exists in each locale. Add missing keys with empty strings (placeholders until translated). The gaps are typically 10-15 keys per locale: new pages not propagated, recently-added content keys, object sub-keys. ### Verification ```bash for loc in es en nl de; do for page in "" faq sobre servicios programas contacto blog privacidad glosario; do curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://site.com/${loc}/${page}" done done ``` ## Asset Activation Policy Nothing gets deleted without client confirmation. Every dormant asset is an unactivated feature. Keep all `@ai-whisperers/*` packages installed (tree-shaken at build time, zero runtime cost). Keep unreferenced images on disk and in the manifest (pre-generated for future campaigns, <1MB total). #### Content JSON Integrity Check (Pre-Deploy) Before building the Docker image, validate ALL content JSON files. A corrupt JSON makes SSR return `content: {}` (empty pages) with zero errors in Docker logs: ```bash python3 -c " import json, os for f in os.listdir('content'): if f.endswith('.json'): try: json.load(open(f'content/{f}')) except json.JSONDecodeError as e: print(f'CORRUPT: {f} — {e}') exit(1) print('All content JSON valid') " ``` ### Stale .next/ directory pitfall After `git pull origin main` that changes content JSON files or source code, the existing `.next/` directory may be stale. The Docker `COPY . .` includes `.next/`, so stale build output + new content = empty SSR pages. Fix: ```bash rm -rf .next/ # Only when source/content files changed npm run build # Rebuild from scratch ``` After any content edit or page config change, validate all section content keys resolve: ```python import json, os with open('content/es.json') as f: content = json.load(f) for fname in os.listdir('nexa-pages'): cfg = json.load(open(f'nexa-pages/{fname}')) for sec in cfg.get('sections', []): ck = sec.get('content', sec['id']) resolved = content for part in ck.split('.'): if isinstance(resolved, dict) and part in resolved: resolved = resolved[part] else: resolved = None; break if resolved is None: print(f"MISSING: {fname} → {sec['id']} → {ck}") ``` ## Client Site Deep-Dive — Full Business Assessment When a client site already exists with docs (like Nexa Paraguay), don't just look at code — produce a structured assessment that covers ALL areas the business owner needs to see. ### Assessment Structure 1. **Code & Content Audit** — browse the live site + examine repo structure + read all docs 2. **Competitive Position** — price positioning, AI readiness, niche focus (use existing docs if they exist) 3. **Financial Model Review** — unit economics, funnel estimates, cost structure (which docs exist) 4. **Gap Analysis** — what's broken now (P0/P1) vs what needs client input 5. **Phased Roadmap** — organize into 6 phases by impact: (1) Fix broken pages, (2) Conversion path, (3) Trust & credibility, (4) SEO & analytics, (5) Multi-language, (6) AI differentiation ### Quick-Win Batch Sizing Always include a "quick-wins I can execute right now" section with concrete, batch-fixable items: - Fix page config `id` fields across all page JSONs - Add sitemap.xml + robots.txt - Replace fabricated stats with honest text - Fix blog image key mismatches - Remove `is_demo` flag - Add unique meta titles per page - Enable additional locale routing ### Business Model Impact Table Show the before/after for the client in financial terms they understand: | Metric | Current | With improvements | |---|---|---| | Clients/month | X | Y | | Avg ticket | $Z | $W | | Monthly revenue | $A | $B | ### Key Questions to Answer - What's the AI differentiation opportunity? (Check competitor landscape — most have zero AI) - What justifies the premium pricing? (AI layer, multilingual, faster process) - What's the head start window? (Usually 12-18 months before competitors catch up) - What are the 3-5 blog posts that would dominate zero-competition keywords? ## Pitfalls See `references/post-deployment-audit-pattern.md` for the systematic audit process to run after every deployment. - **Factory-demo content ships by default**: Fabricated stats (+500 families, 98% success), AI headshots, fake testimonials — these are NOT placeholders the client filled in, they're the scaffold. Replace them before anyone sees the site. Check `is_demo` flag in site.json.**: The `template-nextjs-client` has 52 files that must be removed for B2B. Missing any will cause build failures. Run the transformation checklist above. - **@ai-whisperers package versions**: Always resolve version mismatches before building. Use `GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token)` for npm install. - **Network name**: Always use `agent-net` for Swarm stacks. Verify with `docker network ls | grep overlay`. - **Build output**: The Next.js `output: 'standalone'` config is required for Docker builds. Set in `next.config.ts`. - **Blog data structure**: Template expects `blog.posts[]`. If your content has flat `blog[]`, adapt both `app/blog/page.tsx` and `app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx`. - **docker-compose.yml stale values**: The template's compose file has `superspuma` hardcoded in image name and Traefik labels. Replace ALL references. - **Content JSON has Fun4Me remnants**: The template pre-populates `content/es.json` with erotic/Fun4Me products, images, blog posts, brand references, Instagram handles (@fun4me_py), and a `brand` section. Overwrite completely with client content. Also check these files for hardcoded Fun4Me values: - `components/share-whatsapp.tsx` — hardcoded "Mirá esto de Fun4Me:" text - `components/article-json-ld.tsx` — hardcoded `fun4me.paragu-ai.com` URL - `components/breadcrumb-jsonld.tsx` — hardcoded `fun4me.paragu-ai.com` URL - `components/faq-json-ld.tsx` — reads `item.q`/`item.a` but content has `question`/`answer` - `app/rss.xml/route.ts` — "Fun4Me Store" title and "bienestar íntimo" description - `app/contacto/page.tsx` — Quick links for order status, returns, shipping; Instagram @fun4me_py; hardcoded Fun4Me phone - `app/nosotros/page.tsx` — "¿Por qué Fun4Me?" section with sex shop story; reads `c.brand` instead of `c.nosotros` - **Dead index files**: After removing components, check `lib/hooks/index.ts` and similar index files for dangling re-exports. - **AnalyticsProvider API**: `@ai-whisperers/seo` v0.2.0 has a different API than the template expects. The `AnalyticsProvider` component from the package may be empty or mismatched. If build fails, remove the import and use raw GA4 script tag instead. - **DNS can't be configured from this environment**: Cloudflare API token not available. Must add A records through the Cloudflare web dashboard. - **No prices on B2B sites**: B2B products are custom-quoted. Never show prices. Use "Request quote" CTAs instead of "Buy now". - **WhatsApp as primary CTA**: For B2B in Paraguay, WhatsApp is the dominant business communication tool. Every page should have a WhatsApp float + at least one WhatsApp CTA section. - **Mobile sticky WhatsApp bar**: Add `components/mobile-cta.tsx` and include in layout. Add `pb-20 md:pb-0` to body className for spacing. - **Docker build with GITHUB_TOKEN**: Using `--build-arg GITHUB_TOKEN` triggers Docker BuildKit secret warnings and fails. Instead use `Dockerfile.prod` that copies the prebuilt `.next/standalone` directory and runs `node_modules/.bin/next start` — no npm install at Docker build time. Build on the host first, then Docker just packages output. - **CI/CD reusable workflow failures**: The `Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd` reusable workflows may not be accessible from new repos due to org permissions. Use a standalone `deploy.yml` that SSHes to the VPS and runs the deploy commands directly. This avoids the ci-cd access issue entirely. - **FAQ JSON-LD format mismatch**: If FAQ data uses `question`/`answer` keys but the JSON-LD component reads `q`/`a`, it silently fails to render FAQ schema. Fix by updating the component's key access. - **RSS feed inherits template owner**: The template's `app/rss.xml/route.ts` has hardcoded site name, description, and base URL. Always update all three for the new client. - **All JSON-LD components hardcode fun4me URL**: Every `*-json-ld.tsx` component needs its base URL updated. Read from `content.site.url` instead of hardcoding. ", "path": "professional-services-website/SKILL.md", "skill_dir": "/root/.hermes/skills/professional-services-website", "linked_files": {"references": ["references/data-misalignment-detection.md", "references/json-page-driven-architecture.md", "references/client-site-launch-checklist.md", "references/b2b-site-from-template.md", "references/section-components-pattern.md", "references/content-duplication-audit-pattern.md", "references/gateway-popup-pattern.md", "references/nexa-paraguay-content-reference.md", "references/pages-router-audit-checklist.md", "references/client-business-assessment-template.md", "references/client-market-intelligence-analysis.md", "references/post-deployment-audit-pattern.md", "references/multi-locale-activation.md", "references/docker-build-with-file-deps.md", "references/golden-visa-mvp-pattern.md", "references/whatsapp-sales-agent-deployment.md", "references/paragu-ai-builder-tenant-analysis.md", "references/nexa-paraguay-standalone-migration.md", "references/[REDACTED_TOKEN].md", "references/pitchy-blindex-worked-example.md"]}, "usage_hint": "To view linked files, call skill_view(name, file_path) where file_path is e.g. 'references/api.md' or 'assets/config.yaml'", "required_environment_variables": [], "required_commands": [], "missing_required_environment_variables": [], "missing_credential_files": [], "missing_required_commands": [], "setup_needed": false, "setup_skipped": false, "readiness_status": "available"}
