{"success": true, "name": "nextjs-content-audit", "description": "Audit and fix Next.js content-driven sites where JSON content exists but doesn't render. Covers page config → section component mapping, generic fallback renderers, data key standardization, theming, and architectural cleanup for content-heavy Next.js Pages Router sites.", "tags": ["nextjs", "content", "audit", "architecture", "section-components", "theming", "standardization"], "related_skills": [], "content": "--- name: nextjs-content-audit description: "Audit and fix Next.js content-driven sites where JSON content exists but doesn't render. Covers page config → section component mapping, generic fallback renderers, data key standardization, theming, and architectural cleanup for content-heavy Next.js Pages Router sites." version: 1.0.0 tags: [nextjs, content, audit, architecture, section-components, theming, standardization] --- # Next.js Content Audit & Standardization ## When to Use A content-heavy Next.js site where: - JSON content files exist (content/es.json, images.json) but pages show empty shells - Generic fallback renderer shows only headings, ignores items/paragraphs/members/pillars - 25+ page configs reference sections with no matching component - Data key names vary across pages (q/a vs pregunta/respuesta vs term/definition) - Theming uses inline hex values repeated 200+ times - No caching, no error boundaries, 50+ `: any` types ## Detection ### 1. Content-Exists-but-Not-Rendering Audit ```bash # For each page config, check if the content key actually resolves cd /root/project for f in nexa-pages/*.json; do slug=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$f')); print(d.get('slug','unknown'))") python3 -c " import json d_content = json.load(open('content/es.json')) d_config = json.load(open('$f')) for sec in d_config.get('sections',[]): content_key = sec.get('content','') if content_key: parts = content_key.split('.') obj = d_content for p in parts: obj = obj.get(p, {}) if isinstance(obj, dict) else None status = 'OK' if obj else 'MISSING' print(f'{sec[\"id\"]:20s} → {content_key:30s} [{status}]') " 2>/dev/null done ``` ### 2. Component-to-Section Mapping Gap Analysis ```bash # Extract all section IDs from page configs grep -roh '"id": "[^"]*"' nexa-pages/*.json | sort -u | while read line; do id=$(echo "$line" | cut -d'"' -f4) if ! grep -q "'$id'" src/pages/\\[slug\\].tsx src/pages/index.tsx 2>/dev/null; then echo "MISSING COMPONENT: $id" fi done ``` ## Core Architecture Pattern ### Three-tier rendering pipeline: ``` Page Config (nexa-pages/*.json) → SECTION_MAP (src/pages/[slug].tsx) → Section Component (src/components/sections*.tsx) → Content JSON (content/*.json, images.json) ``` ### Section Component Contract Every section component should accept these props: ```tsx interface SectionComponentProps { variant?: string pageContent: Record // reconstructed as {keyName: data} data?: any // the resolved section data (preferred) images?: any // images.json manifest [key: string]: any } ``` Component pattern: ```tsx export function MySection({ pageContent, data, images }: SectionComponentProps) { const d = data || pageContent || {} // data is preferred const items = d.items || [] if (!items.length) return null // early return if empty return (
...
) } ``` ### Section Dispatch (in page file) — CRITICAL PAGE CONTEXT FIX The dispatch must reconstruct `pageContent` so that components looking up by content key name get the correct data. **Do NOT pass `sectionData || content` as pageContent directly** — section components look up `pageContent.hero`, not `pageContent` as the hero data itself. ```tsx const Component = SECTION_MAP[section.id] if (Component) { const sectionData = resolveContent(content, section.content || section.id) const pc: any = {} const keyName = section.content?.split('.').pop() || section.id pc[keyName] = sectionData || content return } ``` The `keyName` extracts the last segment of the content key (e.g. `home.hero` → `hero`, `faqPage.full` → `full`) which matches what the component looks up. This pattern must be applied in BOTH `[slug].tsx` AND `index.tsx` — they share the same dispatch logic but are separate page files. ### Generic Fallback Renderer For sections without dedicated components, render content generically: - `data.items || data.full?.items || data.groups || data.pillars || data.members || data.paragraphs || data.trust?.items` - `data.body || data.content` - Headline, subheadline, eyebrow, CTA button - Image resolution for memberImage and imageUrl ### Content Duplication Detection When a dedicated page (e.g. `/servicios`) renders the same service/program content as the homepage, the issue is usually **content data duplication** in the JSON files, not in the rendering logic: 1. `home.services` and `servicesPage.services` both define the same 3 groups and 9 services 2. The page configs reference different content paths (`home.services` on homepage, `servicesPage.services` on servicios page) 3. Each resolves correctly, but the content under those paths is structurally identical **Detection:** ```bash # Compare two content paths from the JSON python3 -c " import json d = json.load(open('content/en.json')) h = d.get('home',{}).get('services',{}) s = d.get('servicesPage',{}).get('services',{}) h_groups = [g['title'] for g in h.get('groups',[])] s_groups = [g['title'] for g in s.get('groups',[])] print('home.services groups:', h_groups) print('servicesPage.services groups:', s_groups) print('SAME:', h_groups == s_groups) " ``` **Full cross-page scan for all section types:** ```python def content_signature(obj): \"\"\"Create a compact structural signature for comparison.\"\"\" if isinstance(obj, dict): keys = sorted(k for k in obj.keys() if k not in ('seo', 'ctaHref', 'image', 'imageUrl', 'icon')) return tuple((k, content_signature(obj[k])) for k in keys) elif isinstance(obj, list): return tuple(content_signature(o) for o in obj[:3]) return str(obj)[:60] # Check two content paths for identical structure h_sig = content_signature(d['home']['services']) s_sig = content_signature(d['servicesPage']['services']) print('IDENTICAL:', h_sig == s_sig) # True means the data is the same under different paths ``` Also scan for **same-component intersections**: which section IDs appear across multiple pages, and which of those have identical content signatures. ```python section_usage = {} for page_id, config in all_pages.items(): for sec in config.get('sections', []): sid = sec.get('id') if sid not in section_usage: section_usage[sid] = [] section_usage[sid].append(page_id) # Then filter to len >= 2 ``` **Fix options:** - **Option A: Remove from one page** — Remove the section from the homepage; the dedicated page covers it with more detail - **Option B: Differentiate content** — Make the homepage version show fundamentally different content (icons/benefits summary vs detailed ✓ lists), using the same keys but shorter descriptions - **Option C: Component-level dedup** — If the same component renders from different content paths but the data is identical, have the dedicated page config reference the homepage's content path directly ### Dedicated section types to always check for duplication | Section ID | Typical duplicate pattern | Fix preference | |---|---|---| | `services` / `services-detail` | Homepage 9-card grid vs /servicios detailed page | Remove from homepage | | `process-timeline` | Homepage 5-step timeline vs /proceso | Remove from homepage or replace with teaser | | `programs-comparison` | Homepage 3-4 tier cards vs /programas | Remove from homepage | | `cta-banner` | Same "Take the first step" CTA across 6+ pages | Make each page's CTA text unique | | `trust-signals` | Same "One program/One trip" across 3+ pages | Differentiate content per page | | `story` | Same "Our Story" on /sobre and /fundador | Give founder a personal bio instead | | `newsletter-signup` | Same form on /blog and /recursos | Acceptable — intentional redundancy | | `why-destination` | Same 3 pillars on home and dedicated | Minor — usually different enough | ## Data Key Standardization ### Common content structures in Spanish JSON files | Content Type | Keys (preferred) | Keys (fallback) | |-------------|-----------------|-----------------| | FAQ items | `q`, `a` | `pregunta`, `respuesta`, `question`, `answer` | | Glossary | `term`, `definition` | `q`, `a`, `title`, `description` | | Team members | `name`, `role`, `description`, `memberImage` | `image`, `imageUrl` | | Blog posts | `slug`, `title`, `excerpt`, `date`, `image`, `body` | `coverImage`, `author` | | Pillars | `title`, `description`, `imageUrl`, `bullets` | `icon` | | Services | `title`, `description` (grouped in `groups[]`) | `icon`, `price` | | Process steps | `number`, `title`, `description`, `duration`, `image` | `time` | | Stats | `value`, `label` | `number`, `description` | | Privacy items | `q`, `a` | `title`, `body` | | Testimonials | `name`, `quote`, `rating`, `image` | `author`, `text` | | Gallery | `images[{src, alt, caption}]` | `items` | ### Standardize to prefer `q`/`a` for FAQ-like content Spanish content files consistently use `q` and `a` as keys (shorter JSON = smaller bundle). Component fallback should check these first: ```tsx const question = item.q || item.pregunta || item.question || item.title const answer = item.a || item.respuesta || item.answer || item.description || item.body ``` ## Theming Standardization ### Theme object pattern ```ts // src/theme.ts export const theme = { colors: { primary: '#1B2A4A', accent: '#C9A96E', bg: '#F5F5F0', white: '#FFFFFF', text: '#444', textMuted: '#666', border: '#e0e0e0', success: '#10b981', whatsapp: '#25D366', gradient: 'linear-gradient(135deg, #1B2A4A 0%, #2a3f6a 100%)', // ... all colors }, radii: { sm: '8px', md: '12px', lg: '16px', full: '50px' }, shadows: { card: '0 4px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)', sm: '0 2px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)' }, spacing: { section: '4rem 1rem', card: '1.5rem', btn: '0.85rem 2.5rem' }, sizes: { maxWidth: '1200px', contentWidth: '800px' }, } ``` ### Destructure at component file top ```tsx const { colors: c, radii: r, spacing: s, sizes: sz } = theme ``` ### Replace ALL inline hex values ```bash grep -o '#[0-9A-Fa-f]\\{6\\}' src/components/*.tsx | sort -u ``` Every unique hex should have a corresponding key in `theme.colors`. ## Asset Activation (Not Pruning) Policy **Critical user preference:** When dormant assets are found (unused packages, unreferenced images, unwired integrations), **do not recommend deletion**. The correct approach is: 1. Document the asset as "dormant but available" 2. List the activation trigger (what feature requires it, what event activates it) 3. Keep everything — deletion requires explicit client confirmation Create an activation roadmap rather than a pruning report: ```markdown | Asset | Status | Activation Trigger | Effort | |-------|--------|-------------------|--------| | 82 images across 9 categories | On disk, unreferenced | When corresponding section/campaign launches | 0 — already generated | | 8 unused packages | Installed, unimported | When feature requires them | Per-feature | | 3 unused locale files | en.json, nl.json, de.json | When multi-locale routing is implemented | 2-3 days | ``` ### Activation Roadmap Philosophy When asked to "remove unused code" or "prune dormant assets," the correct response is: 1. **Never delete anything without explicit client confirmation.** Everything is potentially useful. 2. **Classify each dormant asset** by what would activate it: - `@ai-whisperers/*` packages → "Keep. Activate per-feature when client needs auth/commerce/i18n/etc." - Unreferenced images → "Keep. Pre-generated and ready for campaign launch." - Unused locale files → "Keep. Key parity audited, ready for routing activation." - Content gaps (team photos, pricing, testimonials) → "Keep placeholders. Needs client input to fill." 3. **Document the activation path** — Don't just say "keep," explain how to activate it when needed. 4. **Placeholders are valid for demo/pre-launch** — Team initials, "Próximamente," demo stats, and AI-generated portraits are acceptable. Flag them clearly in docs as `Status: Draft`. ### Client sign-off required for — Real team photos, real testimonials (GDPR), real pricing, real case studies, domain cutover, and any deletion. ## Documentation Structuring Pattern ### 12-Category Hierarchy When organizing documentation for a client repo, use this hierarchy: ``` 00-architecture/ — Core framework docs (ARCHITECTURE.md, DATA_FLOW.md, ACTIVATION_ROADMAP.md) 01-client/ — Client intelligence, stakeholder Q&A, intake questionnaires 02-site/ — Architecture, audits, DNS, improvement plans, COMPONENT_REGISTRY.md 03-brand/ — Brand guide, design tokens, social assets, image prompts 04-images/ — Image manifest, fallback chain, prompt libraries, placeholder hashes 05-content/ — Locales, blog catalog, editorial calendar, demo content 06-marketing/ — Comparisons, email nurture, FAQs, lead magnets, ads, testimonials 07-seo/ — Keyword strategy (multi-language), content gap analysis 08-integrations/ — HubSpot, Mailchimp, GA4, WhatsApp AI bridge setup guides 09-market-intelligence/ — Solstein analysis, market sizing, competitor landscape, financial models 10-deployment/ — Docker runbook, CI/CD reference, deployment commands 11-launch/ — Pre-launch checklist, launch runbook, go/no-go inventory 12-factory/ — NEW_CLIENT_BOOTSTRAP.md — reusable template for creating new repos ``` ### Consistent File Header Every doc file gets a status header: ```markdown # Title **Status:** Current | Draft | Review | Deprecated **Last validated:** YYYY-MM-DD **Scope:** One-line description ``` ### doc Triage Workflow 1. **Audit existing flat files at docs/ root** — identify orphan files (no category home) 2. **Move each flat file** into its category (e.g. `STANDARDIZATION.md` → `00-architecture/`) 3. **Apply status headers** to every existing file — use `Draft` for aspirational features (not yet deployed), `Current` for live features, `Review` for items needing client validation, `Deprecated` for obsolete 4. **Update `docs/README.md`** with full tree, cross-reference table, and stats 5. **Update `CLAUDE.md`** to reference the new structure 6. **Remove old flat files** from root after migration ### Batch via Subagent For large documentation reorganization (20+ files), delegate 3-4 parallel subagents: - Subagent A: Status headers + flat file moves - Subagent B: Cross-reference table + README.md update - Subagent C: Claude.md update + git operations - Subagent D: Create new architecture/core docs (00-architecture/) ### Content Field Alignment (Not Deletion) When a component expects `memberImage` but the content has `imageUrl` — **alias, don't require a rewrite**: ```python # In the content JSON, add the expected field as an alias for m in members: if not m.get('memberImage') and m.get('imageUrl'): m['memberImage'] = m['imageUrl'] ``` Same pattern for glossary (`q` → `term`, `a` → `definition`), process steps, any content field mismatch. ### Multi-Locale Routing Pattern For adding locale support to an existing Pages Router site: 1. **Middleware** — detect locale from cookie → Accept-Language → default. Redirect bare `/` to `/{locale}/`. 2. **`[slug].tsx` → `[[...slug]].tsx`** — rename the catch-all file to optional catch-all so it handles `/en/faq` style paths (slug becomes `['en', 'faq']`, not a 404). 3. **Locale extraction in getServerSideProps**: ```ts export function getServerSideProps({ params }: any) { let slug = Array.isArray(params?.slug) ? params.slug.join('/') : (params?.slug || 'home') let locale = 'es' const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] if (slug.includes('/')) { const parts = slug.split('/') if (LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { locale = parts[0]; slug = parts.slice(1).join('/') || 'home' } } else if (LOCALES.includes(slug)) { locale = slug; slug = 'home' } const fullContent = loadJSON(contentDir, `${locale}.json`) // ... rest of props } ``` 4. **Remove `index.tsx`** — `[[...slug]]` handles `/` (empty array = home). Having both causes route conflict errors at build time ("cannot define the same specificity"). 5. **Locale JSON parity** — audit all locale files against the primary (es.json) and fill missing keys. - Actual gaps per locale: usually small (11-14 keys out of hundreds) - Common gaps: new page sections not propagated (prensa), recently added footer fields, seo.home entries ## Architectural Improvements (Batch) 1. **Fix content → component mapping** (unlocks empty pages) 2. **Standardize data keys** (removes fragile fallback chains) 3. **Extract theme tokens** (eliminates 200+ duplicated hex values) 4. **Remove buildPageContent** (dead code generating unused objects on every request) 5. **Add shared JSON loader with cache** (60s TTL Map cache on readFileSync — deduplicate from 3 copies in page files) 6. **Add ErrorBoundary** (prevents 500 white screen on single section crash) 7. **Mobile nav** (hamburger button was `display: none`) 8. **Footer social links** (LinkedIn, Instagram from site.json) 9. **Remove unmapped sections** (whatsapp-float, compliance-disclaimer, pills) 10. **Clean unused imports and packages** (8+ unused `@ai-whisperers/*` deps) 11. **Add TypeScript types** (replace 48+ `: any` with proper interfaces per content type) ### Image rendering workflow Images on content-driven Pages Router sites use a two-step resolution: 1. `images.json` manifest defines all available images with metadata (src, width, height) 2. Content files reference images via `@img:section.subsection` or `@src:section.key` syntax ```ts // src/components/content.ts export function resolveImage(images: any, ref: string): string { if (!ref || !images) return '' if (ref.startsWith('@img:') || ref.startsWith('@src:')) { const key = ref.replace(/@(img|src):/, '') const parts = key.split('.') let obj = images for (const p of parts) obj = obj?.[p] return obj?.src || obj?.srcSvg || '' } return ref } ``` **Key insight:** The `images.json` file must be included in the Docker build (`COPY images.json` in Dockerfile). Missing this is the most common reason images don't render in production even though they work in dev. ## Interactive Component Patterns ### FAQ with Search For FAQ pages with 20+ items, build a searchable accordion: ```tsx export function FaqSearchSection({ data }: SectionComponentProps) { const d = data || {} const allItems = d.items || [] const [open, setOpen] = React.useState(null) const [search, setSearch] = React.useState('') const items = search ? allItems.filter((item: any) => { const q = (item.q || '').toLowerCase() const a = (item.a || '').toLowerCase() return q.includes(search.toLowerCase()) || a.includes(search.toLowerCase()) }) : allItems return (

{d.title}

setSearch(e.target.value)} />

{items.length} de {allItems.length} preguntas

{items.map((item, i) => ( ))}
) } ``` Register in SECTION_MAP, then update the page config's FAQ section to use `id: "faq"` (maps to `FaqSearchSection`). ### Contact Form with API Endpoint Build a functional contact form that posts to a Next.js API route: ```tsx export function ContactFormSection({ data }: SectionComponentProps) { const d = data || {} const [form, setForm] = React.useState({ nombre:'', email:'', telefono:'', pais:'', servicio:'', mensaje:'' }) const [sent, setSent] = React.useState(false) const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => { e.preventDefault() const res = await fetch('/api/contact', { method:'POST', headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:JSON.stringify(form) }) if (res.ok) setSent(true) } if (sent) return

- **Compose deploy: Traefik labels at service-level**: Must be under `deploy.labels`, not top-level `service.labels`. Docker Swarm ignores service.labels.", "new_string": "- **Next.js 16 middleware deprecation**: `middleware.ts` still works but warns. Use `proxy` in next.config instead.
- **Compose deploy: Traefik labels at service-level**: Must be under `deploy.labels`, not top-level `service.labels`. Docker Swarm ignores service.labels.
- **Blog post JSON structure**: `posts.json` may be `{ "posts": [...] }` not a flat array. Always access via `list = data.posts || data`.
- **`new Date()` in Server Components with PPR**: Counts as uncached data. Hardcode static values (e.g. `'2026'` not `new Date().getFullYear()`).
- **PPR + generateStaticParams must return 1+ results**: If no blog posts exist, still return at least one valid param or the build crashes with `EmptyGenerateStaticParamsError`.
- **`generateMetadata` for dynamic routes with PPR**: Must be synchronous and cannot read filesystem/DB. For blog/slug pages where metadata depends on the content, return static fallback metadata and let the layout title serve.
- **Section-extra components may use `useRouter` from `next/router` for locale**: This crashes PPR prerendering. Replace with locale prop. The SectionsRenderer already passes `locale={locale}` — use it."}

{"action": "write_file", "name": "professional-services-website", "file_path": "references/case-study-section-pattern.md", "file_content": "# Case Study Section Pattern

A reusable section component for professional-services client sites that displays client testimonials with structured detail views.

## When to Use

- Site needs to showcase client success stories with more depth than a simple quote grid
- Multiple case studies exist (5+), and users should browse between them
- Each story has: quote, before/after comparison, key metrics (savings, milestones), and stats

## Architecture

### Data Schema (`testimonials.json`)

```json
{
  "testimonials": {
    "title": "Casos de éxito",
    "subtitle": "Historias reales de personas que hicieron de Paraguay su hogar.",
    "stats": {
      "totalClients": 5,
      "satisfactionRate": 100,
      "averageRating": 5,
      "countries": ["Países Bajos", "Alemania", "Bélgica", "España"]
    },
    "items": [
      {
        "name": "Johan van der Berg",
        "location": "Países Bajos → Paraguay",
        "rating": 5,
        "quote": "The single sentence that captures the story. Keep under 200 chars.",
        "program": "business",
        "date": "2026-03",
        "featured": true,
        "verified": true,
        "image": "testimonials/client-name.jpg",
        "extraDetails": {
          "industry": "IT Consulting",
          "incomeRange": "120k-180k",
          "beforeState": "Their exact words describing the problem before Nexa.",
          "afterState": "Their exact words describing life after.",
          "savingsAmount": 48000,
          "savingsCurrency": "EUR",
          "keyMilestone": "Bank account opened in 48 hours",
          "biggestBenefit": "time"
        },
        "beforeAfter": {
          "before": "Short sentence. Powerful.",
          "after": "Short sentence. Contrast."
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

### Data Loading

Inject `testimonials.json` into the content object in the data loader:

```typescript
// In lib/page-data.ts or equivalent server data layer
const images = JSON.parse(readFileSync(join(REPO, 'images.json'), 'utf-8'))
try {
  const testimonials = JSON.parse(readFileSync(join(REPO, 'testimonials.json'), 'utf-8'))
  content.testimonials = testimonials.testimonials
} catch {}
```

This makes the testimonials data available at `content.testimonials` for any page to reference.

### Page Config

```json
{
  "sections": [
    { "id": "page-hero", "content": "caseStudiesPage.hero" },
    { "id": "case-studies", "content": "testimonials" },
    { "id": "cta-banner", "content": "caseStudiesPage.cta" }
  ]
}
```

The section ID `case-studies` maps to `CaseStudySection` in the registry.

### Component Structure

The component has three visual sections:

1. **Stats bar** — Full-width colored section with 4 stats: total clients, satisfaction %, average rating, countries of origin
2. **Active case study** — Name selector buttons (pill-style), followed by a card with:
   - Avatar (initials circle), name, location
   - Star rating
   - Blockquote of the testimonial
   - Before/After comparison (2-column grid)
   - Badge row: savings amount, key milestone, program name
3. **CTA** — "Share your story" link to submission form

### Registration in SectionsRenderer

```typescript
'case-studies': dynamic(() => import('./sections-extra').then(m => ({ default: m.CaseStudySection })), { ssr: true }),
```

### Content Key Resolution

The section's `content` field (`"testimonials"`) resolves via `resolveContent(content, "testimonials")`, which gets the `testimonials.testimonials` object injected by the data loader. The component receives this as its `data` prop.

## Client Submission Form

Create a standalone HTML form at `/public/testimonios/caso-de-exito-form.html` for clients to submit their own stories:

```
14 questions across 3 sections: profile, experience, consent
Posts to /api/contact with type: 'testimonial'
Has success state, graceful error fallback
Shareable via WhatsApp link
```

## Collection Questionnaire

For interviewing clients, use `docs/06-marketing/case-study-questionnaire.md`: 25 questions over 5 sections (profile, before state, experience, after state, consent). Collect via WhatsApp voice notes or 15-min video call.

## Pitfalls

- **Data must be injected, not imported**: `testimonials.json` is a separate file from the locale content JSON. The data loader must explicitly read and inject it into the content object. Without this, the resolveContent chain breaks.
- **Stats data is optional**: The stats bar section is guarded by `stats.totalClients > 0` checks. If stats are null/0, that stat block simply doesn't render.
- **Before/After is optional**: Not every testimonial needs before/after. Guard with `active.beforeAfter &&`.
- **Featured item as default selection**: If no item has `featured: true`, selected defaults to 0 (first item). Always mark at least one as featured.
- **Form is client-side only**: The HTML form is a static file in `public/`. It makes a POST to the API. If the API fails, it still shows success to not frustrate the user.
"}

{"action": "write_file", "file_content": "# Conversion Components: Booking Form & Exit Popup

Reusable conversion-focused components for professional-services sites.

## Multi-Step Booking Form (`BookingFormSection`)

A 3-step wizard: program selection → contact details → review/submit.

### Architecture

The component is self-contained in a single file. It does not need external state management — all state is local `useState`.

```typescript
import { BookingFormSection } from '../components/BookingFormSection'
```

### Steps

| Step | UI | Purpose |
|------|----|---------|
| 1 — Program | 3-4 program cards with price + duration, highlight popular | Qualification — match user to program |
| 2 — Contact | Name, email, WhatsApp, optional message | Data collection for follow-up |
| 3 — Confirm | Summary of program + contact info, submit button | Review before sending |
| Success | Checkmark animation, confirmation text, "back to site" link | Clear completion |

### Program Data

Hardcoded in-component as `PROGRAMS` array. Each entry:
```typescript
{ id: 'business', title: 'Residencia + Empresa + Banco', subtitle: 'Business', price: '€4,500', duration: '12-16 semanas', popular: true }
```

### Content Props

The component accepts `data` with all text labels for i18n:
- `data.title`, `data.subtitle`
- `data.step1Title`, `data.step1Subtitle`, `data.step2Title`, `data.step2Subtitle`, `data.step3Title`, `data.step3Subtitle`
- `data.nameLabel`, `data.emailLabel`, `data.phoneLabel`, `data.messageLabel`, `data.messagePlaceholder`
- `data.submitLabel`, `data.backLabel`, `data.nextLabel`
- `data.confirmTitle`, `data.confirmText`, `data.backCta`

If not provided, Spanish defaults apply.

### API Integration

On submit, POSTs to `/api/contact` with `type: 'booking'` and all form data. Fails silently if the API is down (shows success anyway).

### Page Config

```json
{ "id": "booking-form", "content": "bookingForm" }
```

Register in SectionsRenderer:
```typescript
'booking-form': dynamic(() => import('./BookingFormSection').then(m => ({ default: m.BookingFormSection })), { ssr: true }),
```

## Exit-Intent Popup (`ExitPopup`)

Triggers when the user tries to leave the page (mouse leaves top 5px of viewport) or after 30s idle. Captures email in exchange for a lead magnet.

### Triggers

1. **Mouse-leave**: `document.addEventListener('mouseleave', handler)` — fires when `event.clientY <= 5`
2. **Time-based**: `setTimeout` at 30s

Only fires once per session (localStorage `nexa-exit-popup` key prevents re-display).

### Content Props

Accepts `data` with:
- `data.title`, `data.subtitle` — the hook
- `data.placeholder` — email input placeholder
- `data.ctaLabel` — download button text
- `data.confirmTitle`, `data.confirmText` — post-submit state
- `data.closeLabel` — "No, gracias" link
- `data.disclaimer` — GDPR-compliant privacy note

### API Integration

POSTs to `/api/contact` with `type: 'exit-popup'` and `email`. Silent failure.

### Layout Integration

Use a wrapper component that provides default content:

```typescript
// ExitPopupWrapper.tsx
'use client'
import dynamic from 'next/dynamic'
const ExitPopup = dynamic(() => import('./ExitPopup').then(m => m.ExitPopup), { ssr: false })

export default function ExitPopupWrapper() {
  return <ExitPopup data={{ title: 'default', ... }} />
}
```

Mount in root `layout.tsx`:
```typescript
<body>
  <ExitPopupWrapper />
  {children}
</body>
```

## Pitfalls

- **Both components must be 'use client'** — they use `useState`, `useEffect`, event handlers
- **ExitPopup must be `{ ssr: false }`** — it accesses `window` and `document` directly
- **Booking form needs `.then(m => ({ default: m.BookingFormSection }))`** in dynamic imports since the component is a named export
- **ExitPopup localStorage guard**: Check on mount that the user hasn't already submitted or dismissed the popup — prevents re-showing after page navigation
- **ExitPopup focus**: The popup uses `fixed inset-0 z-50` with `backdrop-blur-sm` to block the background and focus attention on the email capture
- **Program prices are hardcoded**: For multi-locale sites, prices should be locale-aware. Currently they're hardcoded in euros.
", "file_path": "references/conversion-components.md", "name": "professional-services-website"}

Covers page config → section component mapping, generic fallback renderers, data key standardization, theming, and architectural cleanup for content-heavy Next.js Pages Router sites." version: 1.0.0 tags: [nextjs, content, audit, architecture, section-components, theming, standardization] --- # Next.js Content Audit & Standardization ## When to Use A content-heavy Next.js site where: - JSON content files exist (content/es.json, images.json) but pages show empty shells - Generic fallback renderer shows only headings, ignores items/paragraphs/members/pillars - 25+ page configs reference sections with no matching component - Data key names vary across pages (q/a vs pregunta/respuesta vs term/definition) - Theming uses inline hex values repeated 200+ times - No caching, no error boundaries, 50+ `: any` types ## Detection ### 1. Content-Exists-but-Not-Rendering Audit ```bash # For each page config, check if the content key actually resolves cd /root/project for f in nexa-pages/*.json; do slug=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$f')); print(d.get('slug','unknown'))") python3 -c " import json d_content = json.load(open('content/es.json')) d_config = json.load(open('$f')) for sec in d_config.get('sections',[]): content_key = sec.get('content','') if content_key: parts = content_key.split('.') obj = d_content for p in parts: obj = obj.get(p, {}) if isinstance(obj, dict) else None status = 'OK' if obj else 'MISSING' print(f'{sec[\"id\"]:20s} → {content_key:30s} [{status}]') " 2>/dev/null done ``` ### 2. Component-to-Section Mapping Gap Analysis ```bash # Extract all section IDs from page configs grep -roh '"id": "[^"]*"' nexa-pages/*.json | sort -u | while read line; do id=$(echo "$line" | cut -d'"' -f4) if ! grep -q "'$id'" src/pages/\\[slug\\].tsx src/pages/index.tsx 2>/dev/null; then echo "MISSING COMPONENT: $id" fi done ``` ## Core Architecture Pattern ### Three-tier rendering pipeline: ``` Page Config (nexa-pages/*.json) → SECTION_MAP (src/pages/[slug].tsx) → Section Component (src/components/sections*.tsx) → Content JSON (content/*.json, images.json) ``` ### Section Component Contract Every section component should accept these props: ```tsx interface SectionComponentProps { variant?: string pageContent: Record // reconstructed as {keyName: data} data?: any // the resolved section data (preferred) images?: any // images.json manifest [key: string]: any } ``` Component pattern: ```tsx export function MySection({ pageContent, data, images }: SectionComponentProps) { const d = data || pageContent || {} // data is preferred const items = d.items || [] if (!items.length) return null // early return if empty return (
...
) } ``` ### Section Dispatch (in page file) — CRITICAL PAGE CONTEXT FIX The dispatch must reconstruct `pageContent` so that components looking up by content key name get the correct data. **Do NOT pass `sectionData || content` as pageContent directly** — section components look up `pageContent.hero`, not `pageContent` as the hero data itself. ```tsx const Component = SECTION_MAP[section.id] if (Component) { const sectionData = resolveContent(content, section.content || section.id) const pc: any = {} const keyName = section.content?.split('.').pop() || section.id pc[keyName] = sectionData || content return } ``` The `keyName` extracts the last segment of the content key (e.g. `home.hero` → `hero`, `faqPage.full` → `full`) which matches what the component looks up. This pattern must be applied in BOTH `[slug].tsx` AND `index.tsx` — they share the same dispatch logic but are separate page files. ### Generic Fallback Renderer For sections without dedicated components, render content generically: - `data.items || data.full?.items || data.groups || data.pillars || data.members || data.paragraphs || data.trust?.items` - `data.body || data.content` - Headline, subheadline, eyebrow, CTA button - Image resolution for memberImage and imageUrl ### Content Duplication Detection When a dedicated page (e.g. `/servicios`) renders the same service/program content as the homepage, the issue is usually **content data duplication** in the JSON files, not in the rendering logic: 1. `home.services` and `servicesPage.services` both define the same 3 groups and 9 services 2. The page configs reference different content paths (`home.services` on homepage, `servicesPage.services` on servicios page) 3. Each resolves correctly, but the content under those paths is structurally identical **Detection:** ```bash # Compare two content paths from the JSON python3 -c " import json d = json.load(open('content/en.json')) h = d.get('home',{}).get('services',{}) s = d.get('servicesPage',{}).get('services',{}) h_groups = [g['title'] for g in h.get('groups',[])] s_groups = [g['title'] for g in s.get('groups',[])] print('home.services groups:', h_groups) print('servicesPage.services groups:', s_groups) print('SAME:', h_groups == s_groups) " ``` **Full cross-page scan for all section types:** ```python def content_signature(obj): \"\"\"Create a compact structural signature for comparison.\"\"\" if isinstance(obj, dict): keys = sorted(k for k in obj.keys() if k not in ('seo', 'ctaHref', 'image', 'imageUrl', 'icon')) return tuple((k, content_signature(obj[k])) for k in keys) elif isinstance(obj, list): return tuple(content_signature(o) for o in obj[:3]) return str(obj)[:60] # Check two content paths for identical structure h_sig = content_signature(d['home']['services']) s_sig = content_signature(d['servicesPage']['services']) print('IDENTICAL:', h_sig == s_sig) # True means the data is the same under different paths ``` Also scan for **same-component intersections**: which section IDs appear across multiple pages, and which of those have identical content signatures. ```python section_usage = {} for page_id, config in all_pages.items(): for sec in config.get('sections', []): sid = sec.get('id') if sid not in section_usage: section_usage[sid] = [] section_usage[sid].append(page_id) # Then filter to len >= 2 ``` **Fix options:** - **Option A: Remove from one page** — Remove the section from the homepage; the dedicated page covers it with more detail - **Option B: Differentiate content** — Make the homepage version show fundamentally different content (icons/benefits summary vs detailed ✓ lists), using the same keys but shorter descriptions - **Option C: Component-level dedup** — If the same component renders from different content paths but the data is identical, have the dedicated page config reference the homepage's content path directly ### Dedicated section types to always check for duplication | Section ID | Typical duplicate pattern | Fix preference | |---|---|---| | `services` / `services-detail` | Homepage 9-card grid vs /servicios detailed page | Remove from homepage | | `process-timeline` | Homepage 5-step timeline vs /proceso | Remove from homepage or replace with teaser | | `programs-comparison` | Homepage 3-4 tier cards vs /programas | Remove from homepage | | `cta-banner` | Same "Take the first step" CTA across 6+ pages | Make each page's CTA text unique | | `trust-signals` | Same "One program/One trip" across 3+ pages | Differentiate content per page | | `story` | Same "Our Story" on /sobre and /fundador | Give founder a personal bio instead | | `newsletter-signup` | Same form on /blog and /recursos | Acceptable — intentional redundancy | | `why-destination` | Same 3 pillars on home and dedicated | Minor — usually different enough | ## Data Key Standardization ### Common content structures in Spanish JSON files | Content Type | Keys (preferred) | Keys (fallback) | |-------------|-----------------|-----------------| | FAQ items | `q`, `a` | `pregunta`, `respuesta`, `question`, `answer` | | Glossary | `term`, `definition` | `q`, `a`, `title`, `description` | | Team members | `name`, `role`, `description`, `memberImage` | `image`, `imageUrl` | | Blog posts | `slug`, `title`, `excerpt`, `date`, `image`, `body` | `coverImage`, `author` | | Pillars | `title`, `description`, `imageUrl`, `bullets` | `icon` | | Services | `title`, `description` (grouped in `groups[]`) | `icon`, `price` | | Process steps | `number`, `title`, `description`, `duration`, `image` | `time` | | Stats | `value`, `label` | `number`, `description` | | Privacy items | `q`, `a` | `title`, `body` | | Testimonials | `name`, `quote`, `rating`, `image` | `author`, `text` | | Gallery | `images[{src, alt, caption}]` | `items` | ### Standardize to prefer `q`/`a` for FAQ-like content Spanish content files consistently use `q` and `a` as keys (shorter JSON = smaller bundle). Component fallback should check these first: ```tsx const question = item.q || item.pregunta || item.question || item.title const answer = item.a || item.respuesta || item.answer || item.description || item.body ``` ## Theming Standardization ### Theme object pattern ```ts // src/theme.ts export const theme = { colors: { primary: '#1B2A4A', accent: '#C9A96E', bg: '#F5F5F0', white: '#FFFFFF', text: '#444', textMuted: '#666', border: '#e0e0e0', success: '#10b981', whatsapp: '#25D366', gradient: 'linear-gradient(135deg, #1B2A4A 0%, #2a3f6a 100%)', // ... all colors }, radii: { sm: '8px', md: '12px', lg: '16px', full: '50px' }, shadows: { card: '0 4px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)', sm: '0 2px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)' }, spacing: { section: '4rem 1rem', card: '1.5rem', btn: '0.85rem 2.5rem' }, sizes: { maxWidth: '1200px', contentWidth: '800px' }, } ``` ### Destructure at component file top ```tsx const { colors: c, radii: r, spacing: s, sizes: sz } = theme ``` ### Replace ALL inline hex values ```bash grep -o '#[0-9A-Fa-f]\\{6\\}' src/components/*.tsx | sort -u ``` Every unique hex should have a corresponding key in `theme.colors`. ## Asset Activation (Not Pruning) Policy **Critical user preference:** When dormant assets are found (unused packages, unreferenced images, unwired integrations), **do not recommend deletion**. The correct approach is: 1. Document the asset as "dormant but available" 2. List the activation trigger (what feature requires it, what event activates it) 3. Keep everything — deletion requires explicit client confirmation Create an activation roadmap rather than a pruning report: ```markdown | Asset | Status | Activation Trigger | Effort | |-------|--------|-------------------|--------| | 82 images across 9 categories | On disk, unreferenced | When corresponding section/campaign launches | 0 — already generated | | 8 unused packages | Installed, unimported | When feature requires them | Per-feature | | 3 unused locale files | en.json, nl.json, de.json | When multi-locale routing is implemented | 2-3 days | ``` ### Activation Roadmap Philosophy When asked to "remove unused code" or "prune dormant assets," the correct response is: 1. **Never delete anything without explicit client confirmation.** Everything is potentially useful. 2. **Classify each dormant asset** by what would activate it: - `@ai-whisperers/*` packages → "Keep. Activate per-feature when client needs auth/commerce/i18n/etc." - Unreferenced images → "Keep. Pre-generated and ready for campaign launch." - Unused locale files → "Keep. Key parity audited, ready for routing activation." - Content gaps (team photos, pricing, testimonials) → "Keep placeholders. Needs client input to fill." 3. **Document the activation path** — Don't just say "keep," explain how to activate it when needed. 4. **Placeholders are valid for demo/pre-launch** — Team initials, "Próximamente," demo stats, and AI-generated portraits are acceptable. Flag them clearly in docs as `Status: Draft`. ### Client sign-off required for — Real team photos, real testimonials (GDPR), real pricing, real case studies, domain cutover, and any deletion. ## Documentation Structuring Pattern ### 12-Category Hierarchy When organizing documentation for a client repo, use this hierarchy: ``` 00-architecture/ — Core framework docs (ARCHITECTURE.md, DATA_FLOW.md, ACTIVATION_ROADMAP.md) 01-client/ — Client intelligence, stakeholder Q&A, intake questionnaires 02-site/ — Architecture, audits, DNS, improvement plans, COMPONENT_REGISTRY.md 03-brand/ — Brand guide, design tokens, social assets, image prompts 04-images/ — Image manifest, fallback chain, prompt libraries, placeholder hashes 05-content/ — Locales, blog catalog, editorial calendar, demo content 06-marketing/ — Comparisons, email nurture, FAQs, lead magnets, ads, testimonials 07-seo/ — Keyword strategy (multi-language), content gap analysis 08-integrations/ — HubSpot, Mailchimp, GA4, WhatsApp AI bridge setup guides 09-market-intelligence/ — Solstein analysis, market sizing, competitor landscape, financial models 10-deployment/ — Docker runbook, CI/CD reference, deployment commands 11-launch/ — Pre-launch checklist, launch runbook, go/no-go inventory 12-factory/ — NEW_CLIENT_BOOTSTRAP.md — reusable template for creating new repos ``` ### Consistent File Header Every doc file gets a status header: ```markdown # Title **Status:** Current | Draft | Review | Deprecated **Last validated:** YYYY-MM-DD **Scope:** One-line description ``` ### doc Triage Workflow 1. **Audit existing flat files at docs/ root** — identify orphan files (no category home) 2. **Move each flat file** into its category (e.g. `STANDARDIZATION.md` → `00-architecture/`) 3. **Apply status headers** to every existing file — use `Draft` for aspirational features (not yet deployed), `Current` for live features, `Review` for items needing client validation, `Deprecated` for obsolete 4. **Update `docs/README.md`** with full tree, cross-reference table, and stats 5. **Update `CLAUDE.md`** to reference the new structure 6. **Remove old flat files** from root after migration ### Batch via Subagent For large documentation reorganization (20+ files), delegate 3-4 parallel subagents: - Subagent A: Status headers + flat file moves - Subagent B: Cross-reference table + README.md update - Subagent C: Claude.md update + git operations - Subagent D: Create new architecture/core docs (00-architecture/) ### Content Field Alignment (Not Deletion) When a component expects `memberImage` but the content has `imageUrl` — **alias, don't require a rewrite**: ```python # In the content JSON, add the expected field as an alias for m in members: if not m.get('memberImage') and m.get('imageUrl'): m['memberImage'] = m['imageUrl'] ``` Same pattern for glossary (`q` → `term`, `a` → `definition`), process steps, any content field mismatch. ### Multi-Locale Routing Pattern For adding locale support to an existing Pages Router site: 1. **Middleware** — detect locale from cookie → Accept-Language → default. Redirect bare `/` to `/{locale}/`. 2. **`[slug].tsx` → `[[...slug]].tsx`** — rename the catch-all file to optional catch-all so it handles `/en/faq` style paths (slug becomes `['en', 'faq']`, not a 404). 3. **Locale extraction in getServerSideProps**: ```ts export function getServerSideProps({ params }: any) { let slug = Array.isArray(params?.slug) ? params.slug.join('/') : (params?.slug || 'home') let locale = 'es' const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] if (slug.includes('/')) { const parts = slug.split('/') if (LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { locale = parts[0]; slug = parts.slice(1).join('/') || 'home' } } else if (LOCALES.includes(slug)) { locale = slug; slug = 'home' } const fullContent = loadJSON(contentDir, `${locale}.json`) // ... rest of props } ``` 4. **Remove `index.tsx`** — `[[...slug]]` handles `/` (empty array = home). Having both causes route conflict errors at build time ("cannot define the same specificity"). 5. **Locale JSON parity** — audit all locale files against the primary (es.json) and fill missing keys. - Actual gaps per locale: usually small (11-14 keys out of hundreds) - Common gaps: new page sections not propagated (prensa), recently added footer fields, seo.home entries ## Architectural Improvements (Batch) 1. **Fix content → component mapping** (unlocks empty pages) 2. **Standardize data keys** (removes fragile fallback chains) 3. **Extract theme tokens** (eliminates 200+ duplicated hex values) 4. **Remove buildPageContent** (dead code generating unused objects on every request) 5. **Add shared JSON loader with cache** (60s TTL Map cache on readFileSync — deduplicate from 3 copies in page files) 6. **Add ErrorBoundary** (prevents 500 white screen on single section crash) 7. **Mobile nav** (hamburger button was `display: none`) 8. **Footer social links** (LinkedIn, Instagram from site.json) 9. **Remove unmapped sections** (whatsapp-float, compliance-disclaimer, pills) 10. **Clean unused imports and packages** (8+ unused `@ai-whisperers/*` deps) 11. **Add TypeScript types** (replace 48+ `: any` with proper interfaces per content type) ### Image rendering workflow Images on content-driven Pages Router sites use a two-step resolution: 1. `images.json` manifest defines all available images with metadata (src, width, height) 2. Content files reference images via `@img:section.subsection` or `@src:section.key` syntax ```ts // src/components/content.ts export function resolveImage(images: any, ref: string): string { if (!ref || !images) return '' if (ref.startsWith('@img:') || ref.startsWith('@src:')) { const key = ref.replace(/@(img|src):/, '') const parts = key.split('.') let obj = images for (const p of parts) obj = obj?.[p] return obj?.src || obj?.srcSvg || '' } return ref } ``` **Key insight:** The `images.json` file must be included in the Docker build (`COPY images.json` in Dockerfile). Missing this is the most common reason images don't render in production even though they work in dev. ## Interactive Component Patterns ### FAQ with Search For FAQ pages with 20+ items, build a searchable accordion: ```tsx export function FaqSearchSection({ data }: SectionComponentProps) { const d = data || {} const allItems = d.items || [] const [open, setOpen] = React.useState(null) const [search, setSearch] = React.useState('') const items = search ? allItems.filter((item: any) => { const q = (item.q || '').toLowerCase() const a = (item.a || '').toLowerCase() return q.includes(search.toLowerCase()) || a.includes(search.toLowerCase()) }) : allItems return (

{d.title}

) } ``` Register in SECTION_MAP, then update the page config's FAQ section to use `id: "faq"` (maps to `FaqSearchSection`). ### Contact Form with API Endpoint Build a functional contact form that posts to a Next.js API route: ```tsx export function ContactFormSection({ data }: SectionComponentProps) { const d = data || {} const [form, setForm] = React.useState({ nombre:'', email:'', telefono:'', pais:'', servicio:'', mensaje:'' }) const [sent, setSent] = React.useState(false) const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => { e.preventDefault() const res = await fetch('/api/contact', { method:'POST', headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:JSON.stringify(form) }) if (res.ok) setSent(true) } if (sent) return
